Subject: CI Event - Recording, Slides and Resources – Developing Custom GPTs for CI 
 

Dear Kevin,

I’ve received a request for access to today’s CI CoP event, Developing Custom GPTs for Continuous Improvement.

 

The recording, presentation slides and transcript from the session are now available here <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-strategy-governance-planning/SitePages/Events.aspx#developing-custom-gpts-for-continuous-improvement>  along with recordings of our previous CI events.

 

A huge thank you to Vincent Bryce (Head of Continuous Improvement, Faculty of Law) and Lyndsey Middleton (Lead Business Analyst, IT Services) for sharing their knowledge, expertise and practical insights with the CI Community. Their engaging demonstration helped demystify custom GPTs and showed that creating useful AI-powered tools is often less about technical expertise and more about understanding users, defining a clear purpose and making thoughtful design decisions.

During the session, Vincent and Lyndsey explored what custom GPTs are, how they can support Continuous Improvement activities, and the key considerations involved in designing, sharing and maintaining them. Together, attendees helped create a prototype CI-focused GPT, demonstrating how AI can be used to make knowl

emails.subject: College Staff Structure
emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Anne Mortimer; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Steve McBrearty; Kevin Lelitte
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

I have been made aware of Katherine Corr’s message below:

 

Can I please check; does this impact the way we have configured the structure of Company 90 in PeopleXD? The proposed structure would introduce new Entity 2 codes, which will either be in this format: 



Or this format:



 

Level 2 codes map directly to Management Unit in PeopleXD, and we have set this up as follows:



 

Katherine’s message seemed to suggest that this change would need to happen so that college staff could be recorded in PeopleXD, but I’m not sure that’s really true is it? Especially as we have already set up a structure which is different to their proposal.

 

I’m not going to lie, I think the proposal in general is long overdue, I have no idea why we keep colleges at level 2 and don’t pad the structure so that equivalent entities sit at the same level, but I’m just wanting to understand the impact on what we’re doing if this goes ahead – would you expect us to amend the structure to align with the level 2/management unit codes that PACS are using? And what would that mean for the rest of the structure? Where would we split colleges and permanent private halls?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.we

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD - Weekly Team Meeting notes - 23-Jun-26
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Simon Burford; Steve McBrearty; Kevin Lelitte
emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting earlier in the week. Apologies for the delay in getting these to you. Please note actions.

 

*	Simon explained that there are three types of appointment which can be used: 1)Normal standard post 2) Auto-authorized post 3) Auto-commenced post
*	Anne stated a preference for the auto ones since these allowed for the creation of buckets and generally offer more flexibility. The auto route is currently used for casuals, TSS and non-employees.  Action: AM to give some thought about what buckets are wanted – what parameters and generic roles are needed. Before deciding however, it was recommended that Action: AM to undertake the training first. 
*	Action: SM - Simon noted the need to consult Access Group on whether we needed planned appointments to do the data loads or whether we can load directly into a bucket post.
*	Personal Number sequencing was discussed. All entries in C90 will be given a new number, and it was agreed that there should not be a separate numbering system for C90. This means that a mechanism needs to be found for ensuring that numbers are not double allocated. There already exist PXD ‘no input days’ and so it was suggested that we could use these periods to do the imports.
*	It was agreed that we should start preparing dummy data ready for testing. Action: NK to prepare some dummy data. 
*	Simon confirmed that the environment is built with hierarchy structure in place - but we are awaiting the creation

emails.subject: RE: Owner Required - Task# 50945166 for ServiceReq# 30404938
emails.body:
Hi Kevin,

 

I had previously queried this with Michael, please see response below – I will go back to the user that raised it.

 

I’ve reviewed this and ran TIMDEP03 Sickness Absence Reasons across the full University. When filtering the results to show records where the Sickness End Date is blank, the report returns 165 open‑ended sickness absences. In these cases, the Length of Sickness Episode (Days/Hours) displays as 0, which is expected behaviour for an absence that has not yet been closed.

 

TIMDEP04 Absence Reasons has been intentionally designed to return absences within a defined date range, using a “Start Date From” and “End Date To” parameter. Because of this structure, absences with no end date fall outside the logic of the report and will not be returned. Although TIMDEP04 includes sickness as part of its broader absence coverage, its primary purpose is to report on other leave types such as family leave and other leave.  

 

For sickness‑specific reporting, TIMDEP03 is the designated report and will return both closed and open‑ended sickness absences, as per the example extract below:

emails.subject: Minutes – Cority Product User Group (PUG), 16 June 2026
emails.body:
Hi Chris,
Please find below the minutes from the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting held on Tuesday 16 June 2026.
Minutes
1. Purpose of the Group

*	This was the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting. The group has been set up to bridge the gap between project go-live and longer-term governance, and to provide a forum for users to raise issues, review backlog items, and agree priorities collaboratively.
*	It was clarified that the PUG is intended to be an operational, short-term, system-focused group, distinct from a future POG, which would be more strategic and aligned to PFST workstreams.

2. Membership

*	Membership covers the main operational user groups across Occupational Health and HR Systems, including representation from administration, nursing, advisors, operational management, and HR Systems.

3. Terms of Reference

*	The group agreed the following broad principles: 

	*	collective responsibility for identifying issues and solutions
	*	active participation from members
	*	collective decision-making on priorities
	*	members should highlight impacts on their own operational areas

*	It was also noted that while user priorities should drive the work, HR Systems will continue to advise on feasibility, effort, dependencies, and sequencing.

4. Enhancement Lifecycle

*	The enhancement lifecycle was reviewed as: Enhancement Request → Backlog → Roadmap → Development → Go Live.
*	Enhancement requests are normally raised through O

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Hi Conor,

 

Just to update you, we met today and made a proposal for the post design. The project team would ideally like to set up a number of bucket posts, with hierarchy information completed down to level 4, post title completed and potentially category and/or sub-category completed, with the other fields left to fill in based on specific records being set up. Is it possible to set up a bucket post without specifying the Post Type?

 

We tend to use auto-authorised posts but I see there’s also an auto-commenced post as an option – I am assuming that this would just cut out the need to commence the appointment when the bucket post is used?

 

Are you able to confirm if there’s any restrictions with using bucket posts when it comes to loading the data into the system? Would we have to manually assign post sequence numbers when loading the employee records or is it enough to just include the post number and the data load will automatically populate the sequences and appointment IDs?

 

The business team are going to go away and confirm the structure of the bucket posts they want, but some of the decisions may depend on what the answers in this email are!

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

emails.subject: Business rules for Data Warehouse
emails.body:
Hi Ewan,

 

In our meeting this morning we agreed that I would confirm the rules that would need to be put in place to ensure that only University data is being processed in the current data warehouse and the future Fabric solution. Separately we will tackle the best way of getting Company 90 data into a separate area of the warehouse.

 

It was proposed that we would likely need to pull all of the data into the data warehouse and then use logic within the bronze layer to ensure we’re only surfacing and processing further data from within the University domain. The logic for how we can approach this is as follows:

 

Employee & Appointment Related Tables

In order to ensure only University data in passed into our data warehouse solution we need to restrict appointment level data to any appointment IDs which meet the following rules:

*	Company = 10 
*	The sum of days a company 10 appointment held, by person_reference > 1

 

We can then use this group of employees and appointments to restrict other tables which aren’t directly linked to appointment data (for example, person level information, UDF information, etc).

 

Examples of tables which will need to link appointment_id to the logic table in order to filter on company 10 appointments only:

[PEOPLEHR].[CT_BALANCE_ADJUSTMENT]

[PEOPLEHR].[CTBI_PERSON_CALCULATED_HOURS]

[PEOPLEHR].[PABI_PERSON_PENSION_SCHEME]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_USER_DATA_ATITL]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_COST_ALLO

emails.subject: Escalation Request – Case 69001655
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I would like to request your assistance in escalating Case 69001655.
The issue was initially raised on 11 June regarding an error within Recruitment E-Form Maintenance. When attempting to save changes within the Search Appointments Version 4 configuration, the system redirects to a "Sorry But Page Not Found" error and the changes are not saved.
Maura McGlynn has been helpful throughout the case and was able to reproduce the issue before raising it with Development on 12 June. Since then, I have continued to seek updates as the issue is preventing us from making required configuration changes.
On 15 June, I requested an update and was advised that the changes could potentially be applied manually. On 16 June, I provided the requirements document and details of the specific changes required. I also confirmed that the same changes had already been successfully implemented elsewhere, demonstrating that the configuration itself is valid and that the issue appears to be specific to saving changes within this section of Recruitment.
On 18 June, I advised that the matter had become time-critical as we have a delivery deadline and are unable to complete testing until the issue is resolved. I also requested that a Teams call be arranged so that the issue could be reviewed directly. The latest update advises that Development has been given the requirements, however we have not received any information regarding the investigation, root cause, ownership, workaround, or expe

emails.subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM) and process change for managing service request approvals
emails.body:
From: Louise Piper, ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist

To: All OSM users

 

Please disregard this email if you do not manage Service Requests.

Note that any previous recommendations have been updated.

 

For Information: 

•                        Access to IT Self Service has been partially restored for staff and students

	*	Can log their own requests
	*	‘My Items’ is currently unavailable

•                        A temporary change to the Service Request Approval process is in place

•                        Internal users of OSM can no longer log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

 

Dear All,

 

Access to IT Self Service has been partially restored for staff and students. Staff and students can log their own Service and Support Requests (SR) within IT Self Service as normal, however ‘My Items’ is not yet available.

 

As a result, Service Request Approvals cannot currently be progressed through the usual approach, and the alternative process below should be used.

 

To ensure that SRs can continue to progress without delay, approval notifications have been updated to include full details of the request, and to ask approvers to reply directly with their approval vote. Once a response has been received, the SR will turn yellow to indicate it has been updated.

It is no longer necessary to send a manual email requesting approval.

 

It is no longer possible for internal users of OSM to log SRs on

Hi Michael, 

 

Hope that you are well I have some information on this and have the below comms :

 

Thank you for reaching out. We are aware that you recently received an automated email notification from OnboardingExperience@theaccessgroup.com <mailto:OnboardingExperience@theaccessgroup.com>  about a support ticket.

 

We can confirm this email was sent in error as a result of an internal systems testing activity. The ticket has been closed, and no action is required from you.

This was not a hacking incident, and your account and data have not been compromised. The email originated from our own systems and was triggered unintentionally during development work on a new integration feature.

 

We sincerely apologise for any concern this may have caused. If you received multiple such emails, please be assured that all of them relate to the same testing error and none require your attention.

 

If you have any further questions please do not hesitate to contact us.

 

Regards, 

Sarah

Hi all,

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 17 June 2026 11:05
To: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: P5 Task assigned to you - Task# 50960484 for Incident# 11687334

 

Just because I can't resist adding a thought...

Wasn't the original scope intended to include a Talent Admin function sitting within POD (or a similar central team)? I seem to recall discussions that this type of administration wouldn't sit within the HR Systems remit.

Whilst the number of cases is currently low, I wonder whether it's worth revisiting the ownership question before this becomes established as a BAU activity. If there is an expectation that these exceptions will continue to arise, it may be worth considering where responsibility is best placed from a process and resourcing perspective, rather than defaulting it to Support or FA.  

 

Best wishes,

Michael

emails.subject: Holiday Records Reporting – FlexPoints Approval
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

I wanted to give you an update on the Holiday Records reporting work and seek approval to proceed with the associated FlexPoints request.

Following discussions with Athena and Simon, the requirement has been broken down into three Insight reports:

*	
	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year
*	
	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements
*	
	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination


The reports are intended to support our Holiday Records Duty requirements and cover the majority of the reporting requirements that have been identified.

As we were approaching the end of June and the expiry of our remaining FlexPoints, I worked with Athena to pull together the report specifications and sent these to Access for review. Mike was on annual leave at the time, so I copied in Ann-Marie Topliss to see if she could help progress the request or direct it to the appropriate team whilst he was away.

Ann-Marie subsequently raised FlexPoints case 69001638 and advised that, if the work was deemed feasible, approval would be needed before the FlexPoints expire so that the remaining allocation could be safeguarded for this work.

Access have now completed their review and provided a quotation under case 69001638. The quotation has been raised as FlexPoints request FPT-062315 for 2,800 FlexPoints and is currently awaiting approval.
The quotation totals 2,800 FlexPoints in full, made up of 1,750 for the Payroll Consultant element an

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Simon & Kevin,

 

I hope you are both well.

 

Thank you for your time on Friday. Apologies again for the error which we encountered which derailed us for a period.

I spent some time troubleshooting the issue with my colleagues on Friday and there was some data missing from database for Company 30. One of the expected params was blank which was not visible from the front end.

 

We checked all other companies, and this was the only effected one.

 

I have now shared the recordings from Friday with you both.

 

Please find below a breakdown of our call Friday:

 

Key Outcomes

1. Company & Pay Group Setup

*	A new non-payroll company (Company 90) was created to support Colleges and Halls.
*	Approach agreed: 

	*	Copy an existing company and remove unnecessary payroll elements (simpler than building from scratch).

*	A corresponding non-payroll pay group (Pay Group 90) was created: 

	*	Duplicated from a non-employee pay group template.
	*	Linked to Company 90.
	*	Configured with placeholder values (as it will not be used for payroll processing).

________________________________

2. Organisational Hierarchy Build

*	The hierarchy structure was successfully defined and built across: 

	*	Company
	*	Division (e.g. 901 – Colleges & Halls)
	*	Subdivision / Level 4
	*	Management Units (e.g. Permanent Private Halls)
	*	Departments (existing departments reused)

*	Departments were linked using“include linked i

Kevin

emails.subject: PeopleXD Insight Reporting – Quote Request for Holiday Records Reports
emails.to: Mike West; Ann-Marie Topliss; Amanda Mcdonnell
emails.body:
Hi Mike,
I hope you're well.
We've identified a requirement for a number of new PeopleXD Insight reports to support our holiday records reporting requirements and have developed an initial specification for review.
The attached documents include:

*	Oxford Holiday Records Revised Three Report Specification
*	Oxford Holiday Records Report Field Lists

The proposed solution consists of three reports:

*	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year
*	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements
*	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination

We would be grateful if you could review the attached requirements and advise whether the proposed reports are achievable within Insight Reporting, together with an indication of any custom development that may be required.
I appreciate you are currently out of office, so I have copied Amanda McDonnell and Anne-Marie Topliss in case either is able to assist or direct this to the appropriate team while you are away.
We are working to a tight deadline, as we have flex points available within the current year that must be utilised before month end. We would therefore appreciate a quotation or indicative estimate at the earliest opportunity, as our intention is to use the remaining flex point allocation to fund this work if it is deemed feasible.
If a quotation cannot be provided from the attached documentation alone, would it be possible to arrange a scoping session as a priority, ideally within the next week

emails.subject: Re: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports
emails.body:
Hi Athena,
Thank you for sending this through.
I've reviewed the revised spec. The three-report approach provides a much clearer structure than the original proposal, particularly in separating leave entitlement, accrual and holiday pay, and termination payments into distinct reporting areas.
I'll send the specification and field list to Access Group and ask them to review the requirements. Given our current flex point position and the need to utilise any remaining funding before month end, I'll also request an indicative quote as soon as possible. If they are unable to provide this from the documentation alone, I'll ask that a scoping session is arranged as a priority.
I'll let you know once I've heard back from them.
Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up
emails.body:
Good Morning Kevin,

 

I hope you are well.

 

Sorry, I didn’t see your email as I am working down through them, but to cover what I said on Teams:

 

“Yes that is correct in terms of where possible to load, but just to confirm firstly, you do wish for the "REF2029" codes to appear on the employee appointment record similar to what shows for other employees currently., it is user field 5 which can be located on the Data Centre under the Reference Data area.

Yes this would be the correct place to create the codes.

As a test you could create one called "Test" and assign a random code and you should then upon saving be able to see this in the Hierarchy as an available item which to link at 

If you have access on "Hr User Access" for "Personnel" access at any level above "Pay Administered By", you should inherit the access to this area so long as you have the access to the department, level, division etc above it which it is linked to on the Hierarchy.

In terms of Data Migrating the data, yes this is possible, if you use the "Reference - HR Reference File" it is possible to load the reference data en masse. I am blocked from attaching a document to this chat but will send across in an email shortly.”

 

Including above a test file which I just loaded on my own internal site to confirm all worked as expected. In theory you should be able to able to use this file on your UAT site and it should work if you wished to use it as a quick test.

 

If

Working on impact analysis (Person and/or Appt records for a 'new company' 90).

Assumed: current integrations (Biztalk and int-stg) are supposed to only transfer company=10 info (main univ payroll appts).

 

However.. Biztalk currently filters only on appt dept code not starting with 'Z'.

i.e. no explicit `and p.company=10` and/or `and a.company=10` in its query.

Thus company!=10 info may be leaking into subscribing systems.

Further there may be data quality issues around the person and appt tables in pxd.

For Interface-staging pxd flows - they DO restrict on  prbi_hr_person_master.company = 10. 

BUT NOT ON prbi_appointment_histor

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Data in PXD - Integrations
emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Anne Mortimer; Kevin Lelitte; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

 

I’ve responded to Guy – he sent me this (and the context) in an OSM task.

 

For transparency, the full text from Guy is:

 

Working on impact analysis (Person and/or Appt records for a 'new company' 90).

Assumed: current integrations (Biztalk and int-stg) are supposed to only transfer company=10 info (main univ payroll appts).

 

However.. Biztalk currently filters only on appt dept code not starting with 'Z'.

i.e. no explicit `and p.company=10` and/or `and a.company=10` in its query.

Thus company!=10 info may be leaking into subscribing systems.

Further there may be data quality issues around the person and appt tables in pxd.

For both issues the following query shows extent of problem, as at june2026.

 

For Interface-staging pxd flows - they DO restrict on  prbi_hr_person_master.company = 10. 

BUT NOT ON prbi_appointment_history.company_code

(atm there are no appt records with company!=10).

 

Questions: 

1. Given results below, is BT sending appt info to subscribing systems?

 particularly emp 2384116 is a recently ended appt.

2. For all flows, should they be checking person.company only, or appt also (assuming not appt as that appears to be 10 always, though I haven't reviewed the pxd BI layer views to see if they are filtering out rows).

3. Similar to q2, but specifically for the new company 90 - is that going to work like companies 50, 60, 63, 70?

 

And my response to him was:

Crispin

3. Similar to q2, but specifically for the new company 90 - is that going to work like companies 50, 60, 63, 70?

 

 

Could you reach out and/or connect him to people who can elaborate on details?

 

Many thanks,

 

 

Ben Hill

emails.subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus
emails.body:
Hi Marie, 

 

Yes, the automated data upload from PXD to Cardinus is now live and working as expected. Based on initial spot checks, the data appears to be accurate.

 

However, there are a small number of issues that we are addressing as part of BAU:

 

*	SBS users – These users are currently included in the data upload but should not be, as they use a separate DSE system. There is an email rule currently in place to exclude them from DSE emails but ideally we would want them to be excluded from the data upload. We may need to raise a service request to exclude SBS users from the feed. I believe Ralph has raised this with the Azure team. Ralph is currently on annual leave and not back until the 15th June. 
*	Contractors and other non‑PXD users with DSE requirements – As these users are not present in PXD, they are being automatically archived during the weekly upload. As a temporary workaround, these users can be manually tagged as ‘Do Not Archive’ to prevent this. Any new users not in PXD who require DSE access will also need to be created manually. 
*	Users without an ox.ac.uk email address – There are a small number of users without an Oxford email address. We are monitoring these and keeping an eye on them, as the numbers are currently low.

 

Process documentation is currently being updated and will be uploaded to the H&S folder ahead of the handover session with Kevin and Chris this afternoon.

 

The next scheduled data upload is Wednesday 10

Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Just to let you know that go‑live has now been agreed for 3rd June. The initial real data file will be delivered into the test folder, allowing me to carry out some spot checks before it is uploaded into Cardinus.

 

The process will then run on a weekly basis every Wednesday morning.

 

There will be some minimal costs associated with hosting. I’ve asked Tony to copy you into those emails, as I’m not certain which cost centre this would fall under.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

emails.subject: Re: Reference request - Lee Strudwick
emails.body:
Hi Claire,
Thank you for your email.
I can confirm the following information in relation to Lee Strudwick:
Dates of employment
February 2025 – 30 January 2026
Job title and key responsibilities
Senior Functional Analyst
Key responsibilities included:

*	
	Providing second-line support for HR and payroll systems, investigating and resolving complex technical issues.
*	
	Analysing business requirements and implementing system enhancements and configuration changes.
*	
	Coordinating system changes, testing, software releases, and quality assurance activities.
*	
	Working closely with stakeholders across HR, Payroll, Pensions, IT Services, and external suppliers.
*	
	Supporting system compliance with legislative and policy requirements.
*	
	Producing and developing reports to meet business needs.
*	
	Managing incidents, problem resolution, and supplier escalations.
*	
	Contributing subject matter expertise to projects, governance groups, and continuous system improvement initiatives.
*	
	Maintaining system documentation, testing procedures, and user guidance materials.
*	
	Supporting the delivery and ongoing development of the HR Systems service.

Reason for leaving
Contract end.
Key skills, achievements, and attributes in their position held
Lee demonstrated strong analytical and problem-solving skills and was able to investigate and resolve complex system issues effectively. He developed a good understanding of HR business processes and system f

emails.to: Natasha Heaton; Samantha Bateman; Carla Thornley; Stuart Robeson; Andrew Fuller; Marie Cooksey; Kevin Lelitte; Dawn McNish; Beata Krzysztan; Isobel Stokoe; Matthew Castle; Benjamin Hill; Chris Mackie; David Hickman; Roy McKee; Djena Heaton; Sarah Warne; Louise Piper
emails.body:
Dear all,
Thank you, @Natasha Heaton <mailto:natasha.heaton@digital.ox.ac.uk> ,for letting us know. I am sorry to hear that the Product Owner is still off sick, and I hope they make a full recovery soon. While it would certainly be beneficial to have the Product Owner involved in the review, I believe the discussion can still be productive with the team representatives currently invited.
The purpose of the session is to review the temporary support arrangements that were put in place earlier this year (end of January), discuss any concerns or required changes, and agree the approach going forward. As the review has been scheduled well in advance and the relevant teams have already been invited, I would prefer to keep the current date. My main consideration would be ensuring that the relevant teams are represented by the appropriate authority to agree any changes.
The objective is to conclude the review with a clear direction and agreed next steps, rather than defer decisions for further rounds of discussion afterwards. For that reason, I suggest we keep the 30 July meeting in the diary unless there is a significant risk that we would be unable to agree and confirm the way forward on the day.


Kind regards,
 
Viktoriia Vasyliuta



IT Support Architect

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square

Oxford OX1 2JD

Home | IT Help <https://help.it.ox.ac.uk/> 


________________________________

From: Natasha

emails.subject: FW: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM), logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals
emails.body:
Hi All

 

Please see centralised communication update for your information regarding the ongoing OSM issue.

 

The next meeting is on Thursday, hopefully we will be given some resolution dates.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Sarah Warne <sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 02 June 2026 16:36
To: it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk
Subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM), logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals

 

Dear all,

 

For Information:  

*	Note that IT self-service is currently unavailable for some users

•    Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•    Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Further to my email on Friday 22 May about the ongoing security issue affecting IT Self-Service via Oxford Service Manager (OSM), the University is actively working with the technical team at Ivanti to secure a resolution that will enable full restoration of service. This may take some time, given Ivanti is supporting impacted organisations around the world. An update on timelines for further engagement is expected from Ivanti’s support team in the next couple of days. 

 

Ian Teasdale, Director of Digital Assurance, is representing the University on a sector group convened by the HE IT sector body, UCISA, to expediate a resolution at a national level. This has already opened addi

emails.subject: HR Systems support activities in May
emails.to: Brian Jenkins; Sharon Naylor; Chris Williams; Andy Bryson; Paul Kayente
emails.body:
Hi All,

 

See below summary of the activities that have taken place in May in support of your systems. Included is also the latest update in relation to the loss of access to the self-service module of OSM and what that means to us.

 

As always, please feel free to share with your teams.

 

Highlights

 

*	No user access to self-service module of OSM – following the discovery of a potential security vulnerability, IT removed access to the self-service module of OSM for all users. This means that system users cannot submit or check the status of self-service requests they have submitted. Specifically for us, this means that requests to grant access to IRIS, Cority and Healthy Working Plus cannot be submitted in the normal way using the relevant self-service request. Instead, these requests must be submitted by email to our support mailbox hssystemssupport@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:hssystemssupport@safety.ox.ac.uk> . Other IRIS self-service requests are also affected. Odyssey does not currently utilise self-service requests, so there are no changes to the support provided.

At present, there is no defined resolution timeline for this issue. Workarounds are in place to ensure all support requests are recorded on OSM to ensure no request is missed – thanks to Rob his help supporting this.

 

*	Cority email issue – The Occupational Health identified that emails were not being received by clients from the Cority system in early May. This was caused by an

Sent: 27 May 2026 15:16
To: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Clive Poole <clive.poole@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Confirming ERs pension rates for annual Financial Statements -- FINAL

 

Athena

 

I am not sure if you are the right person to say this to, but you should be aware that OSPS contribution rates will be changing from 1 October 2026, so the payroll system will need to be updated. The changes are as follows:

 

*	DB employer contributions going down to 11.8% for all cost plans (schemes 12-17)
*	DC employer contributions going up by 1.5% for all tiers (43 to 48)

 

I know we do payroll for Linacre so it will need to change them and any subsidiary companies for whom we have equivalent scheme codes (if that is what happens).

 

From 1 April 2027 DB employee contributions will go down by 0.5%

emails.subject: GLAM- Botanic Gardens Ticket Office and Ashmolean Security- Rostering requirement
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams; Julie Hickman
emails.body:
Hello, all,

On Tuesday, GLAM HR asked if we could meet with a manager in Botanic Gardens who was struggling with using the system. Today myself and Tracey met with Heidi, and also Harriet Webb from Botanic Gardens Ticket Office. 

During the call, it was established that the Botanic Gardens had not been very forthcoming with staffing data required to set their staff up correctly to use leave and absence in PeopleXD. We were able to identify that the Ticket Office team, have similar requirements and ways of working to the Ashmolean Security team, and would therefore benefit from Rostering implementation.

Unfortunately, we are no longer in a position to support this, and therefore, any future roll outs of Rostering, will need to be picked up by yourselves.

Please may I recommend that in the first instance, Business Change and FAs from HR Systems team meet with the GLAM HR, and establish what fact-find data you need the GLAM HR team to conduct with Ash Security and Botanic Ticket Office, to assess if 1. Rostering is the right solution, and 2. Kick start the activity to get them configured for this set up. 

I will follow up with a handover email to this team and GLAM HR as a project closure task. 

Kind regards,

Anna




Anna Carter-Windle

Business Change Manager

Contractor of AL Training Solutions Ltd

emails.subject: For Information: Logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals
emails.body:
From: Louise Piper, ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist

To: All OSM users

 

Please disregard this email if you do not need to log or manage Service Requests on behalf of customers.

Note that any previous recommendations have been updated as a result of further testing and feedback.

 

For Information: 

•              Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•              Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Dear All,

 

Please find attached guidance on how to log a Service Request (SR) on behalf of a customer, and how to manage SR Approvals whilst IT Self Service is unavailable to many colleagues.

 

*	Log Service Requests on behalf of customers in the Service Request workspace in OSM, in your usual Service Desk Analyst or Enhanced Access role, not through IT Self Service.
*	Follow the guidance relating to the Service Request Customer and Reported by fields.
*	Check whether approval is required and review the guidance for how to manage this by email in the interim.
*	If appropriate, change the owner and set the status to Submitted after logging.
*	Review steps for managing older requests with stuck approvals
*	In the event of an issue, assign a Task to the OSM Administration team from the SR

Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

 

Thanks / Regards

Louise

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Louise Piper
ITSM Self-Service

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

I reran the Insight reports for all LEVELS and SOC values in UOXP (after refreshing the data to ensure the latest version was used).

After noticing that there were fewer SOC records than LEVELS records, I compared the two populations in Excel to identify records present in LEVELS but missing from SOC. I was then able to locate the corresponding SOC values in an older SOC extract. From the investigation, it appears the issue is likely related to the Comments field not having been updated to reflect inclusion in the 2024/25 return. This resulted in the records not appearing as expected, since they only had 2023/24 comments.

I also realised that I previously provided the wrong Appointment IDs — apologies for the confusion.

The records currently missing SOC values for 2024/25 are:

Person Reference           Appointment ID               HESA Code         HESA Code Description

2361315                           266215-1                          211                      Natural and Social Science Professionals

2201600                           217565-1                          231                      Teaching Professionals

For both records, the SOC entries do exist historically and can be evidenced in prior extracts. In each case, there is:

*	a record marked as “Returned 23-24”
*	a corresponding record included in the 2024/25 return extract downloaded from HESA

This suggests the records themselves were not missing, but that the Comments fie

Organisation Structure

Reference Data

Engagement with Access Group

Beth

emails.body:
[Apologies – test guidance notes now attached]

 

Good afternoon,

 

Please find information regarding your testing for Java updates.

 

Oracle have released Java JRE 8 update 491 as part of their Critical Patch Updates for April 2026: https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpuapr2026.html 

 

It is important we test against our business systems before they are rolled out to all managed workstations in your units.  The Endpoint Device Management Services team (EDMS) are currently preparing a packaged install for Windows 11 computers.

 

As per the previous test cycle, JRE 8 update (481), for those of you who are not currently using a CONNECT computer, a CONNECT Windows Virtual Machine will be made available for testing via the Remote Desktop Connection.  EDMS have prepared guidance notes for testers to follow the remote desktop process (attached).

 

Notable Dates:

*	Thursday 21st May - Java Runtime Environment 8 Update 481 will be deployed to the test group.
*	4pm on Wednesday 27th May - please report back any issues by this time. 

 

The proposed rollout to all managed CONNECT computers is:

*	28/05/2026 10:00 - Initiate pilot to a subset of CONNECT Windows 11 computers with an earlier version of JRE 8 installed.
*	04/06/2026 10:00 - If the pilot is successful, rollout to all CONNECT Windows 11 computers with an earlier version of JRE 8 installed.
*	11/06/2026 10:00 - The progress of the rollout will be reviewed, and if all is well

Sarah

emails.subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference
emails.to: Athena Artuso; Marie Cooksey; Sarah Rowles
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

I agree with Athena. And I also wonder whether the financial statements would be a really good example to demonstrate? Without getting into any technical details it’s very easy to see the benefits, not just to our team, but to the process as a whole, by being able to produce more advanced outputs which satisfy auditors and keeping the process visibly simple to follow (you could flash up what the R code looked like and then how pretty and simple the alteryx workflow is)?

 

I like the idea of bringing in another team to demonstrate PowerBI, especially if you could bring in someone like Annette Sandrawich who is doing good things for her division using People Data. We could also feed into our longer term vision of being able to release PowerBI datasets for local PowerBI report writers to use, once we get to that point of maturity.

 

It would be really great if we could get someone to demonstrate how we or someone else uses power automate to improve productivity. Again, Athena maybe your use of PA and Teams Forms to collect consistent data? Or maybe what Pete’s team is doing using their booking system and sending out surveys. Although they might have their own slot? 

 

We’d obviously need to be careful how we frame clockify but I think that could be one of the most beneficial tools for everyone to use as it’s free and easy to use, and really good at helping you identify where to concentrate efforts for continuous improvement when you don’t know w

emails.subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference
emails.to: Marie Cooksey; Simon Burford; Sarah Rowles
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

 

I think those are all sensible suggestions for tools we’ve fully integrated into our work in HR Systems (unlike AI, which we’re still learning to use). The powerful combination of these tools to drive continuous improvement within the team could be the wrap-up commentary for the session – i.e. start with PBI and Alteryx, end with how Clockify demonstrates our massive time-savings after implementing these tools? 

 

I’d be happy to nominate myself or Susan for the Alteryx session – Nathan would also be a good shout, as he’s quite a good demonstrator. 

 

Best,

Athena

emails.subject: Response required by 11am: People Conference
emails.to: Simon Burford; Sarah Rowles; Athena Artuso
emails.body:
Hi All

 

I have been asked to pull together a session at the People Conference. It is still very much a work in progress, but I need to advise today on the initial session description.

 

My brief was:   The conference theme is: Shifting the Dial: Strategy to Impact, with a focus on reflecting on the progress since the launch of the People Strategy.  I was asked to consider how we can build capability across the People profession to deliver impact in a changing landscape such as insights driven decision making. The initial thought is that the session could focus on showcasing how tools like Power Automate and PowerBI can deliver insights to improve decision making, processes, and efficiencies.

 

I don’t want it to be a technical session, I would like it to have more of a workshop, demonstration approach. Primarily demonstrating some of the activity, tools used and achievements within the People Department to give the audience ideas, inspiration, confidence that they can do it to. I also don’t want it to just be me talking at people for 40 minutes.

 

So I am enquiring whether you agree with my suggestions below and names you would propose for demo. Please note this will not just be members of HR Systems I am keen to bring others in from other teams.  

 

I think HR Systems could give a demo of:

*	Clockify – key message that it allows us to identify time-consuming task to drive our focus of attention and demonstrate benefit 
*	Alteryx – workflows

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Simon

 

Thank you for explaining, and yes that seems a logical approach, especially as you can change if administrative responsibilities change.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 15 May 2026 17:53
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Marie,

 

This field indicates who holds responsibility for maintaining the records, which as it stands is the research services teams for REF2029. I wanted to have an indication that it is not the colleges themselves who hold responsibility for the administration of records within the system.

 

It’s only reference data so in 2029 or as and when the administrative responsibility for these records change we can reflect that in the descriptions of the codes. It’s the codes that are actually the most important aspect of it. We wouldn’t report on level 7 / pay admin by either unless we were targeting those responsible for the administration.

 

Hope that clarifies my thinking. I will be running all of the proposed config past you before doing anything with it once I’ve pulled it all together.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting on 12th May: 

 

Sensitive Units

*	Decision: It was agreed that we do not need any sensitive units.

 

Organisation Structure 

*	AM/NK confirmed that they are now happy with the structure and Action: NK will pass the final version to SB.
*	Action: SB – to organize formal sign-off from Marie and then to raise a task with Conor to take forward.

 

Company Code and Naming Convention

*	It is understood that the new company would be ‘90’ but SB/KL will share a more formal proposal on this. Action: SB to share proposal with rest of team before getting Marie to sign-off.
*	Action: SB – on sign-off, SB to add to initiate a Change Request. Note that the CR will include multiple changes relating to this initiative. The CR needs to be signed-off before we go live.

 

User access – visibility of Portal structure and reference data 

*	A question was raised about who would be the guardian for the data and for access. It was assumed that this would rest with AM.
*	Action: AM to confirm how to formalize the role of data guardian being mindful of any college approval needed.

 

Impact Analysis 

*	The impact analysis report (done by Mel Furniss) was shared with the team. Simon has given feedback. It was agreed that we should amend, taking on board feedback, particularly on the risks having appropriate mitigations. Action: CM to ask if Mel can undertake further work.
*	The comment was also made that we

Hi Simon, 

 

Regarding OSM task 50947670 – it’s been a while, but from memory, I think this might be related to two things: the deletion of active reviews, and the bulk deletion of forms assigned to reviewees once the review has been activated.

 

Since the go-live, we’ve encountered cases where departments have mistakenly created and activated reviews, which has caused unnecessary clutter in the system. There have also been instances where incorrect individuals were assigned in large numbers because departments did not select a Department when creating criteria groups.

 

At the moment, it is:

• not possible to delete an active review

• not possible to reset a review to inactive once activated

• not possible to bulk delete assigned reviews (must be done individ

emails.subject: Expressions of interest for HR Systems Support Analyst role
emails.body:
Good morning team,

 

Hopefully you saw my post on the People Teams channel yesterday, we now have permission to replace Otter on a permanent and full-time basis and we are seeking expressions of interest.

 

Please see details below and the attached JD.

 

Julie

 

Expressions of interest for HR Systems Support Analyst role

 

We are seeking expressions of interest for a HR Systems Support Analyst within the HR Systems team.

 

About the role

As an HR Systems Support Analyst, you will engage directly with our diverse user base in a key customer-facing role, addressing technical queries, troubleshooting issues, and providing effective solutions.

 

Key responsibilities

In this role, you will:

*	Provide first-line technical support to HR teams across the University.
*	Work in a fast-paced environment, often under tight deadlines, particularly around payroll deadlines.
*	Collaborate with colleagues to assess, prioritise, and resolve user queries.
*	Escalate complex queries in an efficient manner and to support knowledge enhancement.
*	Identify opportunities to enhance the user experience by reviewing training and guidance materials to encourage self-service, ideally utilising emerging technologies such as AI-powered tools.

For a full list of responsibilities and selection criteria, please reach out to Ffion Fry <mailto:ffion.fry@admin.ox.ac.uk>  to request a copy of the Job Description.

 

Who we’re looking for

We are l

emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Kevin Lelitte; Marie Cooksey
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

 

There is no current DPIA that covers PeopleXD, we are working on one (and have been for about a year) but it’s not been signed off by Marie or information security yet.

 

However, considering this is work that is essentially temporary (data will presumably be kept and updated for a specific and time limited purpose) I would have expected to have a separate DPIA which may refer to the main one but very much focus on the processing of data for this purpose, as the flow of data, the access and maintenance arrangements etc are all different and purposefully separate from the way we process and administer the rest of the system.

 

I’m copying Marie in to this conversation to see if she agrees with me on this one?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

emails.subject: Update on Kevin
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; James Salas Guillen; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Just wanted to drop you a quick email to update you on Kevin now that I’ve caught up on emails etc. 

 

Kevin’s planned surgery was more complex than first thought, he was told that he should recover within a week or two but he has been signed off as not fit for work for four weeks following surgery, so we won’t expect him to be back until 2nd June at least.

 

Work initiatives

I will cover as much of his role as I can. We had been working towards this surgery and so cleared a lot of his workload before he went off but the items I’m aware of as being handed over to me are:

*	the project to bring College staff into PeopleXD
*	the Applicant Successfully Hired change
*	the DPIA for PeopleXD 
*	the ORCID onboarding change (if this comes back to us). 

 

There is a change to the cority applicant data report which Kevin was expecting to pick up on his return, so this may also need to be something I pick up now, depending on what it entails. @James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>  do you have any background you can share to get me up to speed on what needs to happen?

 

@James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> , @Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  & @Asta <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – is there anything else that you were expecting Kevin to take forward? 

 

Meetings

In terms of daily catch ups it would be good for me to attend these on Mondays and Thursdays, on Tuesday and Wednesday I need to lead th

emails.body:

I’ve reached out over whatsapp but just to also let you know that I’m going to send through a referral to occupational health so that I can get some advise on what a return to work should look like, as you may benefit from a phased return or something so just want to make sure you’re properly supported when you are able to return.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Surgery update and medical leave

 

Hi Simon,

 

I wanted to give you an update on my surgery and recovery.

 

The procedure was more complicated than initially anticipated due to issues involving the tendons and scar tissue from the previous joint replacement in the same area, so the surgeon has signed me off for four weeks, I’ve attached the fit note for documentation.

 

I’m due to meet again with the surgeon on May 21 and he has agreed to reassess if I can return to work sooner.

 

I’ll keep you informed of any updates, please feel free to contact me if needed.

 

Best,

Kevin

Hi Marie,

 

Based on the information we now have from Central IT and Cority, this does not appear to have been an ongoing issue. When Cority was initially implemented, it inherited the existing email configuration that had been migrated from Cohort, which was working at the time.

 

Since that implementation, Microsoft and the University have continued to tighten email security controls, particularly around authentication and anti‑fraud (phishing emails) measures. While our outbound emails continued to send successfully from the system, they were not fully authenticated, and once the security checks became stricter, those messages began to be quarantined.

 

In tha

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 08 May 2026 16:02
To: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk>; Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.

 

Hi Rob and Sharon, 

 

We’ve just finished a call with Cority’s technical contact and a member of the University’s Outlook/Nexus team.

 

The issue has now been identified. Outgoing emails from the system were being quarantined by the University’s email security controls. The outgoing emails did not meet the University’s email policy requirements and were automatically held in quarantine rather than delivered to recipients. This explains why the system showed emails as “sent” while recipients did not received them.

 

A total of 1,106 emails sent from enquiries@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:enquiries

emails.body:
Hi Marie and Kevin, 

 

Just to make you aware that we’re currently experiencing an issue where emails are not being sent from the Cority system, which appears to have been occurring since the weekend.

 

This was flagged by Rob yesterday. I’ve raised the issue with both Cority Support and IT Services, as it’s not yet clear whether the root cause sits with the Nexus email relay or Cority’s email configuration.

 

Rob has been informed, and I’ll continue to update him as soon as I receive further information from Cority.

 

At present, the supplier has re‑prioritised the case to a lower priority, which we’re not satisfied with given the impact. I’ve asked for the priority to be reassessed and requested an earlier Teams call, as emails are currently not being sent from the system (last email to the Cority support team below).

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

emails.subject: RE: AD groups for PowerBI reports
emails.body:
Hi Asta,

 

All good questions.

 

This is essentially to replace outlook groups with entra ID security groups, which is the way the University is moving under the identity programme.

 

For context, this is only talking about how we provide security access to view PowerBI reports / dashboards. Similar to the AD groups that control access to Tableau reports but Tableau is being transitioned away from so we should get to the point where it’s only PowerBI reports that we will need to grant access to.

 

So Entra ID Security Groups will control access to view the dashboards, and we’ll still need the CONNECT AD groups to control report developer access to PowerBI (because PowerBI is a connect remove service app). 

 

In the example of the reward team, I imagine that the University Administration and Services - HR Reward PowerBI Admin Outlook group will be replaced by an Entra ID group, which will theoretically make administration of membership and auditing easier. But I’m keeping the total reward team out of scope for the time being because I would imagine they’ll want to set up their own groups to control access to the PowerBI dashboards as Jasmine is taking over ownership of them. 

 

We will continue to control who gets access to the CONNECT\BI-RS-PeopleHR-TotalReward_Report_Writers AD group because this controls access to our area of the data warehouse. 

 

For the teams / areas I’ve outlined in my email we will be setting up Entra ID groups

Hi both,

 

I have reviewed the College Staff in PeopleXD Implementation Proposal which looks good. I have consulted with payroll to make sure there’s no rules we have to adhere to when setting up a payroll company and I’ve also reviewed the existing company and pay group codes. 

 

I propose that we use the code 90 for the company and pay group. There is already a pay group 80 for the OSPS pensioners payroll, so it would be easier to have the company and pay group codes match for this new college area of the system, the existing non-payroll pay group code for the main University is 99 so it will align with keeping non-payroll pay groups in the 90s. 

 

I have updated all references in the proposal document with this code and added reference to the proposed pay group code as well.

emails.subject: Leave Handover — w/c 4 May 2026
emails.body:
Hi Simon,

As discussed at our handover meeting, here’s a summary of the active workstreams for the week. I’ve also linked the full handover document and the DTP1092 proposal below for reference.

DTP1092 — College Staff in PeopleXD
The org structure design session was completed on 27 April. Key decisions are made — we’re reusing the existing management unit structure from Company 10, with Colleges and Halls as two separate units at management unit level. Configuration is estimated at around half a day once the proposal is signed off with Connor O’Brien. Proposal target is w/c 11 May, with sign-off by 15 May.

Actions during my leave:

*	
	Confirm the Company (80) code or choose another to feed into the proposal
*	
	Confirm the pay group name and payroll calendar for Company 80
*	
	Create the new payroll company input in PXD
*	
	Act as point of contact for Melanie Furness (BA) on questions around downstream impact
*	
	Review Nathan and Anne Mortimer’s Excel hierarchy draft when ready


Applicant Successfully Hired Status — PeopleXD (Support Call #11675276)
You’re already leading the Access Group work on this. I’ve raised the change request. On my return I’ll deactivated the status in UOXU and confirm everything is working as expected.

Cority Applicant Data Report
James is managing the manual weekly applicant upload. The date-filtered report still needs rebuilding — happy to pick this up on my return unless you’d like to progress it during the week.

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

With regards the DPIA, then we have now taken compliance advice on this.

 

There is a DPA that covers the data processing element of the existing college census and we are looking to amend this to cover the transfer of data into PXD. The data being loaded into PXD is not sensitive data and therefore does not warrant a DPIA and so will be covered by the amended DPA.

 

Once the data is in PXD however, there will indeed be a need for DPIA coverage in PXD. We were wondering therefore if there is a PXD DPIA which by default covers the processing of the new records we are putting into PXD, or whether an existing PXD DPIA would need amending (most likely)? 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

emails.subject: AD groups for PowerBI reports
emails.body:
Hi Asta,

 

I have been involved in discussions around different types of AD groups as part of the Identity Management programme and I think we need to consider setting up some groups for PeopleXD users so that we can automate the provisioning of access to dashboards such as the Fixed Term Contract dashboard which will need to be locked down to PeopleXD users only.

 

I’d like to make sure we’re aligned with the existing persona’s / roles that we manage at the moment so that we can map any new AD groups to the security matrix to make maintenance as easy as possible, so I just wanted to confirm what I was thinking with you before I took it anywhere.

 

I am thinking we should set up the following…

 

1.	HR Admin PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*         Personnel Administrator 

*         Personnel Administrator Read-Only

*         Staff Request Creator 

*         Staff Request Approver

*         Reporting Only – Personnel

 

2.	Recruitment PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	Recruitment Administrator
*	Recruitment Administrator Read-Only
*	Reporting Only – Recruitment

 

3.	Payroll PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	Payroll Administrator
*	Payroll Manager
*	Payroll Manager + ability to generate Timesheets

 

4.	EDU PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*

emails.subject: RE: Project to get College staff onto peoplexd
emails.body:
Hi Mel,

 

We haven’t got to the level of detail of workgroups yet, and I don’t know if we need to record managers for college staff, but if we do need to record who manages who then we’ll need separate work groups for each team according to the management structure in each college. I also think there’s a risk that using one work group will expose data between different colleges which could be a concern.

 

We do know at this stage that we will have a separate company code for colleges, so any filtering of college data can be done at this level, with the University of Oxford data being held under company 10 and college data being held under a different code (e.g. company 80).

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

emails.subject: Project to get College staff onto peoplexd
emails.body:
Hello Kevin,

I believe Crispin has spoken with you regarding the impact assessment I am producing, for the project looking at putting college staff into PeopleXD.

I just had a quick question and was hoping you might be able to help.  I can see that you are looking to create a separate entity on PXD to put the college staff records into.  Was the option of adding the staff into PXD and putting them into one single workgroup discussed?  I don't have much background of this project or the technical information of how the system is fully configured, so I'm unaware if this was discussed as an option.

If they were added and placed in a single workgroup, I believe they could be in/excluded from reporting where needed?  This would simplify the process and eliminate the need for a separate entity.

I may/probably have missed some of the requirements/technical background which would mean this would not be an option, but if you could let me know if this was looked at as an option, or the reasons why this isn't an option that would be great so I can include it in my report.


Thanks 
Mel

Melanie Furniss
Business Analyst

emails.subject: RE: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD
emails.body:
Thanks Michael, a comprehensive, clear response as always 😊

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 29 April 2026 07:53
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

In PeopleXD, any absence type that employees can request through self‑service must be linked to a entitlement balance. This is because the system needs a measurable unit (days or hours) to validate requests, apply caps, and route them through the standard approval workflow.

 

If Volunteering Days were moved into self‑service, they would therefore require their own dedicated balance and entitlement configuration. The system cannot separate them from annual leave unless a new balance is created and maintained.

 

Because Volunteering Days typically have a fixed allowance, this balance would need its own entitlement rules, its own annual refresh, and its own monitoring. It would also need to be allocated to every relevant employee appointment so that the option appears in self‑service.

 

We have already faced this issue with TOIL.

When reviewing whether TOIL could be booked through self‑service, we reached the same conclusion: enabling self‑service would require creating and maintaining a separate TOIL balance for every employee. The administrative overhead and risk of confusion outweighed the ben

Just taking a very simple look at this using the existing ‘PERDEP01 Staff in Post Full Data Set’ report and filtering on the ‘Employee Sub Status’ for full or part time, so not including term-time, variable hours. This returns:

 

Full‑time vs Part‑time Breakdown

 

•             Full‑time: 18,452 (77.2%)

•             Part‑time: 5,437 (22.8%)

 

So roughly 1 in 4 employees is part‑time.

 

At first glance, Option B looks attractive because it automates carry forward for the majority (full‑time staff). But the critical point is this:

 

The entire complexity and risk sits with the part‑time group, and that group is still very large.

 

5,437 part‑time staff is not a small exception group wh

From: Matt Thomas <matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Subject: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

I hope that all’s well with you.

 

The Volunteering Pilot paper was well received at PCOB yesterday but concerns around the operational complexity associated with logging issues were highlighted as an issue that would need fixing before a wider University roll-out would be possible. 

 

I believe that the main difficulties during the pilot phase were due to a combination of the below:

 

a.	Line managers having to log the hours on behalf of their staff, rather than staff being able to log them and line manager’s approving the hours (as is the case with annual leave);
b.	Some departments still not being fully transitioned onto People XD;
c.	Some departmental administrators still not being fully trained in using People XD.

 

In light of this - I was wondering whether yourself or a member of your team could please kindly provide an update on:

 

1.	The status of departmental migration over to People XD and which departments are still yet to migrate;
2.	The status of training for departmental administrators in using People XD;
3.	The possibility to amend the module so that participants can request their v

emails.subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams; Julie Hickman
emails.body:
Hi Michael

 

Thank you for pulling this together in this easy to digest format.

 

I agree with your recommendation, based on the facts you have presented, but I would like to understand the ratio of FT : PT staff.

 

Michelle & Julie if we go with Michael’s recommendation, how would you propose we communicate this and engage with departments, given that we are aware the WFM project have raised this as a solution going forward?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 27 April 2026 14:14
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration

 

Afternoon, 

 

I’m writing to share the outcome of my review of the PeopleXD carry‑forward functionality, particularly in relation to the pro‑rating of carry‑forward, and to present the recommended approach for this year’s annual leave process. Based on the system’s capabilities and the University’s policy requirements, especially the need to pro‑rate carry‑forward for part‑time staff, my recommendation is to proceed with Option A, continuing with manual HR adjustments. I would welcome your input on this approach before we move forward.

 

I’ll take a look at our calendars and setup a meeting to discuss further.  

 

B

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11675276: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi Kevin,

 

Thanks for this.

 

I’m aware of the issue with records being cleared if they are moved onto a status without the Applicant Acceptance checkbox enabled, this is actually as a result of my testing around identifying records where the this has happened, so I am currently working with the Access Group to identify records where this has occurred so we can script a fix. I would like to get the applicant successfully hired status deactivated before we run that script in LIVE to make sure that we catch all of them and no future ones are introduced.

 

Yes please do raise a change, it doesn’t make sure difference to me which environment you do it in, but UOXU is the most recently cloned so would be sensible to test in there I would think.

 

I don’t believe that there will be any need to provide an alternative status as this is a status that no one should be using. The correct “Final Status” should be applicant accepted, in the scenario where the applicant has been hired.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Support Call# 11675276: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Quick update on the Successfully Hired status — Access Group has confirmed deactivation is the right route. There is an at-risk group to flag, along with a couple of points I need your steer on.
What Access Group confirmed
The retention mechanism is driven by the Applicant Acceptance checkbox on the status setup. If this is unticked, applying the status strips retention. They confirmed the Active toggle is the supported method to deactivate. As an alternative, they suggested ticking Applicant Acceptance on the existing status instead of removing it, which would fix retention while keeping the status usable.
On deactivation

*	Existing records, the Successful Applicants screen, and RECDEP41 are unaffected — deactivation only prevents new use
*	Fully reversible — re-ticking Active restores the status
*	Does not retroactively restore retention

Access Group advised reviewing existing records before cleardown. The at-risk group is anyone on Successfully Hired without a linked HR record (those with a linked record are excluded from cleardown).
Could you pull the count for this group based on your data? We can decide next steps once we have the number. Will departments currently using the status need an alternative? — flagging this for Michelle’s cleardown comms.
Testing plan

*	Apply the status to a test applicant and confirm retention behaviour pre-deactivation
*	Deactivate via the Active toggle and confirm it no longer appears for new use
*	Confirm exis

emails.subject: Organizational Hierarchy Discussion
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Kevin,

 

Thank you for both your own and your colleagues time today.

 

Please find below a summary of the call:

 

1. Executive Summary

*	The session focused on how to incorporate a new non‑payroll company into the existing PeopleXD organisational structure using best practices.
*	Strong guidance was given to manage hierarchy links via the Portal, while continuing to manage hierarchy design via Back Office.
*	Most required reference data already exists; the only potential addition discussed is splitting “Colleges and Halls” at an intermediate hierarchy level.
*	Reuse of existing reference data across multiple companies was confirmed as valid, with reporting implications highlighted.
*	Clear next steps were outlined: confirm reference data decisions, create company/pay group, build hierarchy, then load posts and employees.

________________________________

2. Key Topics Discussed

*	Overview of the current organisational hierarchy in PeopleXD
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy (Portal vs Back Office)
*	Adding a new non‑payroll company into an existing structure
*	Hierarchy levels, indicators, defaults, visibility, and display modes
*	Reference data management (companies, divisions, departments, management units, user fields)
*	Reusing reference data across multiple companies
*	Reporting implications when reference data is shared
*	Payroll-side setup required for a non‑payroll company
*	Pay group creation for non‑payr

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff in PXD - Meeting with Conor Notes - 24th Apr-26
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Nathan Kirwan; Kevin Lelitte; Simon Burford; Nicholas Chandler; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Dear All – please see below the synopsis of the call with Conor:

 

1. Executive Summary 

*	The preferred approach is to incorporate the new non‑payroll company into the existing organisational structure, rather than creating a separate structure.
*	This approach simplifies reporting, security, configuration effort, and use of existing reference data.
*	A new company (e.g. Company 80) will be created within the current structure to hold college/non‑payroll records.
*	Single Sign-On (SSO) and HR self‑service access for individuals with multiple records (company 10 and company 80) remains the key unresolved complexity.
*	Further internal (Oxford-specific) discussion is required on authentication, access precedence, and data maintenance processes.

________________________________

2. Key Discussion Themes and Decisions Made

Organisational Structure Options

*	Two options were assessed: 

	*	Creating a new separate organisational structure
	*	Incorporating a new non‑payroll company into the existing structure

*	Decision direction: 

	*	Strong consensus that incorporation into the existing structure is the best option.
	*	Separate structures introduce complexity for: 

		*	Staff with multiple concurrent appointments
		*	Reporting and analytics
		*	EMS emails, org charts, and manager relationships

Reporting and Analytics

*	Unified structure allows: 

	*	Single, consolidated reporting (Insights / People Analytics)
	*	Avoidance of structure

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11675188: WFM - TUPE Balance - Plymouth University
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

 

I asked Engineering for clarification and they responded:

 

I have been in contact with Helen Burton who was involved in the transfer from Plymouth:

 

The Department will honour the 35-day annual leave and 6 closure days that they have at the University of Plymouth.

 

Whilst transferring staff will retain their existing contractual annual leave entitlements as above, the fixed closure dates during which staff are required to use some of this annual leave for the Department of Engineering will differ from any fixed closure days at the University of Plymouth. The dates of the fixed closure days are available at  https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/fixed-closure-dates <https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/fixed-closure-dates> . These include 5 days at Christmas and 1 day at Easter.

 

So, 35 days of annual leave plus 6 closure days, and, in addition, the bank holidays. Plymouth staff follow the engineering fixed closed days.

 

Have a super sunny weekend!

 

I had a look and it seems their department follows 24Days+8BH+5Xmas+1MThur like UAS.

 

I hope you indeed do have a sunny weekend 😊

 

Best wishes,

Beth

 

Beth Gray

HR Systems Support Team

A-Z of user guides <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/a-z-hr-systems-guidance.aspx>  | Workforce Management resources <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/workforce-management-resources-hr-staff-man

Morning all,

Please find below the agenda for next week’s workshop on Sustaining Leave & Absence Adoption. I’m looking forward to seeing you all online.

We’ll be using the following Miro board during the session:
https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGvJIqfs=/
