When adding your ideas, please keep the focus on sustaining the adoption of leave and absence, rather than reflecting on what has or hasn’t worked well within the project itself.

We’ll also spend time building on this together during the workshop.

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

 

I made sure the Insight report was refreshing data, re-extracted the data and compared to the records for deletion file again:

 

Insight:



 

Extracted UOXU data comments and count:



 

Deletion list comments and counts:



 

So now I can see that all the records for deletion no longer appear, and this is confirmed by the data extracted from Insight showing nulls for all the records for deletion:



 

So I’m not sure what happened yesterday, it might have been something with my laptop since I had that weird excel error I’ve not experienced with appointment id before and I had other laptop issues too, or the Insight report needed refreshing (I thought I had done this!). Anyway, it’s all good now and I’ve set the report to refresh on every change to avoid this happening again.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

 

From: Sarah Rowles 
Sent: 23 April 2026 14:24
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Michael,

 

To confirm the Alteryx workflow wasn’t caching anything because I only created it from scratch when excel was having issues with the datatype of appointment id in the lookup calculation comparing the extract from Insight against the original list for deletion.

emails.subject: RE: Request for funding for Cority
emails.body:
Hi Chris,

 

I couldn’t find anything specific either, but I do remember them sharing a link to their page in the Teams chat (https://www.twilio.com/en-us/sms/pricing/gb). I’ve had a quick look, and it appears Twilio works on a pay‑as‑you‑go model, with no contracts, so we only pay for the number of outbound messages we send.

 

There are a few variables (for example, message length and whether two‑way responses are required, such as Yes/No replies), but as a rough estimate, for around 800 messages per month we would be looking at just over 500 USD per year.

 

We would also need to complete a TPSA (Third‑Party Security Assessment) before implementing this alongside our system.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

emails.subject: Potential Applicant Retention changes
emails.body:
Hi all

 

It has been flagged that there may be a forthcoming change to the retention period of applicant data driven by recent changes to the Employment Rights Act (ERA).

 

Good news: Policy agreed with our request to amend retention period for applicant data from 6-months to 12-months…yay!  But…LSO have since advised that there may be a requirement to review this , due to changes in the length of time for tribunal claim to be raised from 6-12 months.  

 

The ERA will extend the time limit for making an employment tribunal claim from 3 to 6 months.  Together with the elongated period for ACAS early conciliation (extended to 12 weeks in December 2025), this means that a candidate could be issuing a claim 9 months after the conclusion of the applicant process they took part in.  To add to this, the claim could sit in the Employment Tribunal for a number of months before being served on the University. It is unclear if the consideration of offering 14-15 months is driven internally, Oxford being cautious, or if this a new requirement. Kathryn FJ is engaging with LSO for more detail and I will speak to James Bristow Head of DP, for his take on this (he often is the voice of reason on these matters).

 

So, as discussed today we should reign back our engagement campaign until we have better understanding of what the actual ask is for the system and the departments. 

 

Question:

1. If we need to extend retention period of applicant data is that possi

emails.subject: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Could I request that you look into de-activating the “Applicant Successfully Hired” applicant status in PeopleXD?

 

From a very quick look it seems that we can untick the “Active” indicator against this status and this prevents the status from being selectable, but doesn’t seem to error when you view the applicant or anything. However, full testing would be needed, I’m not sure if we have a knowledge article outlining removing applicant statuses?

 

The reason for this is that I have found that departments are fairly regularly using this status but when they do so it removes one of the flags in the background of the database which indicates that the applicant should be retained. When I spoke to the access group recently they asked why we had that status active if it causes us issues. I think it was introduced during an upgrade but I don’t think we have ever asked people to use it and definitely didn’t identify any impact until now, so I’d like to remove it before we switch on the applicant cleardown process.

 

Hopefully it won’t be a large amount of work. I will mention it to Michelle when we talk about applicant cleardown go live comms as it would be good to line these pieces of work up.

 

Please keep me in the loop on who’s going to take this forward.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxfo

emails.subject: Re: Bi-monthly CDR/PD working group
emails.body:
Hi Lindsey,

Thank you for sharing those use cases — they're really helpful in understanding what you're looking to achieve.

I can confirm that automated reminder functionality is available within the Insight module via a feature called iCues. In principle, it would be possible to build reminders along the lines you've described, provided the relevant CDR data is in Insight in a way that can drive the triggers.

That said, this would need to be raised as a formal development request with Access Group, so it can be properly scoped and prioritised alongside our other commitments — my team doesn't currently have the capacity to take it on informally.

Happy to discuss further if that would be useful.

Best, 
Kevin


________________________________

From: Lindsey Spriggs <lindsey.spriggs@socsci.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 15 April 2026 1:41 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mary Muers <mary.muers@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Bi-monthly CDR/PD working group
 

Hi Kevin

 

Thank you for following up on this.

 

In theory, it would be things such as:

 

*	Reminder that a CDR needs to be scheduled (reviewee/reviewer/HR)
*	Reminder that the CDR form needs to be completed (reviewee)
*	Reminder that a CDR form needs to be signed off (reviewee/reviewer/HR)

 

I hope that makes sense – I actually don’t have access to the CDR functionality in My Development, so I can only describe the requirements in the abstract.

 

Best wishes

emails.subject: Outstanding Flexpoints Activities – Payroll Management Training & Payroll Module Health Check
emails.body:
Hi Amanda,

I hope you're well.

I'm writing to follow up on two outstanding items from our agreed Flexpoints package that we are keen to progress as a priority:


1.	
	Payroll Module Health Check (Case No: 67285209 – 2,450 pts) Following our initial Process Review session on 20 October 2025, a follow-up review meeting is still required to complete the review, formalise the findings, confirm conclusions on current behaviour versus best practice, and agree any recommendations or next steps.


2.	
	Payroll Management Training (Case No: 67285342 – 1,050 pts) This training has not yet been scheduled or delivered. We would welcome an initial call to discuss scope and agree dates at your earliest convenience.


When we last spoke, you mentioned being very busy with the Irish payroll - However, both of these items have been outstanding for some time now and we would really like to get them moving.

Could you please let me know your availability over the coming weeks so we can get both sessions in the diary?

I have copied in Mike West for awareness.

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,

This is great, thank you — exactly what I needed. I'll incorporate it into the DPIA.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 4:47 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording 


Hi Kevin,

 

How is this?

 

Data quality is fundamental to the HESA Staff Return, as it underpins the funding, regulation, analysis, and public representation of both the University of Oxford and the UK higher education sector. Accurate staff data informs funding allocations, official statistics, sector league tables, regulatory oversight, and policy development, while also supporting institutional planning and benchmarking. Poor data quality can lead to misallocated resources, distorted metrics, flawed decision-making, and increased regulatory scrutiny, as well as reputational damage. As a core dataset linking to wider sector information, the integrity of the Staff Return is essential to ensuring that decisions across the sector are reliable, transparent, and defensible.

 

In REF 2029, HESA Staff data moves from a supporting dataset to a central mechanism: it determines the scale of submissions will serve as the sector’s authoritative record of research capacity. As a result, staff data accuracy and governance have a direct and material impact on institutional REF outcomes such as how institutions are fun

emails.subject: Re: Freedom of Information requests - DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Athena,

This is really helpful, thank you — I'll incorporate it into the DPIA.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 1:55 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Freedom of Information requests - DPIA wording 


HI Kevin,

 

The short version:

 

The University is obliged to comply with an FOI request only if we already hold the information requested and are able to locate and retrieve it in less than 18 hours and for a cost of <= £450. In addition, there are exemptions from disclosure, which allow a public authority to withhold information in certain circumstances, usually where disclosure is likely to result in some form of harm, which would outweigh the public interest that is presumed to exist in favour of disclosure.

 

The full version:

Personal data held within the University’s HR system (PeopleXD) is subject to the Freedom of Information Act 2000 (FOIA). While the University is required to confirm whether requested information is held and disclose it where appropriate, the majority of HR data engages exemptions under FOIA, particularly where it constitutes personal data.

*	Section 40 (Personal Data) is most commonly applicable, as disclosure must comply with UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018; therefore, identifiable employee information (e.g. names, salaries, sickness records, or performance data) is typicall

emails.body:
Thank you Michael,

 

In Emma’s absence I’ll go back to Andrew.

 

Thank you for the suggested text, I have updated it this morning.

 



 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 16 April 2026 09:05
To: Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Support Call# 11673291: itss - problem with macros in Word

 

Morning Emma,

 

 

Here’s what I think is a suitable response to send back to the Andrew at PCHS, but I’ll leave you to read through and decide whats best.  

 

Thanks for the investigation. Office LTSC 2024 includes Microsoft’s newer macro security model, which blocks macros in files that have a Mark of the Web unless they are stored in a Trusted Location or digitally signed.

The previous guidance (“Disable all macros with notification”) is no longer sufficient for LTSC 2024.

Your workaround of creating a Trusted Location is the correct approach under the new security model. We will update our documentation to reflect this for users on LTSC 2024 or later.

 

 

 

@Julie Hickman <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – At the bottom of this email I have provided a sugges

emails.subject: Re: Halo Case Management (ER,CRM) - Support Model for review and approval
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

To answer your question, the role of HRBPs was discussed and confirmed across several meetings. HRBPs were agreed as the first point of contact for access-related requests, performing the initial review and approval before passing to the CI Squad for implementation. PeopleXD access is assumed as a prerequisite, with any queries raised via the existing route to HR Systems.

Regarding Super Users, this was raised briefly in the November 2025 meeting. The discussion clarified that a Super User is a technical system role for end-to-end testing and is not equivalent to an HRBP role. No agreement was reached on who Super Users would be or how the role would operate in this context.

Having reviewed the document, I would note the following:

The Superuser role is referenced in the product overview but has no corresponding entry in the support structure, enablement requirements, or routing model, unlike the other roles defined in this document.

I wanted to flag this for your awareness.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Viktoriia Vasyliuta <viktoriia.vasyliuta@it.ox.ac.uk>

Subject: Re: Halo Case Management (ER,CRM) - Support Model for review and approval 

Hi Marie,

Thank you for taking you time to review the document.

To Dawn McNish - no, she hasn't come back to me yet.  

And, thank you

emails.subject: ORCIDs in PXD - 14th Apr 26 meeting notes
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Eugenio Barrio; Kevin Lelitte; Simon Burford
emails.body:
Dear All

 

I just want to be absolutely sure that I have correctly understood what we agreed with regards referencing ORCID in PXD. Please can you confirm – is my understanding correct?

 

There are two areas to consider: onboarding and self-service. Currently instructions for ORCID appear only in onboarding and only to a select groups (researchers). There is currently no reference to ORCID (tile or task) in self-service.

It was accepted that capturing ORCIDs directly into PXD, either by free-text inputs or a dynamic connection to ORCID.org, would not be feasible, at least not in the immediate term. It was therefore agreed that the DTP1092 team would:

*	Review/update the current text appearing in the onboarding pages
*	Review/update the links (e.g. adding  https://register.it.ox.ac.uk/self/orcid)
*	Review/update the select groups – most likely expand the groups.

 

Making the changes would be relatively straight forward and is estimated to take 1-2 weeks.

 

Current Onboarding 

 

There is a tile visible to selected groups with instructions and links – see attached example.

 

Currently open to selected groups (are these the correct ones? This list comes from an earlier meeting we had on ORCIDs):

 

*	AC  Academic (teaching & research) 
*	AP  Research Staff (including E grades) 
*	AR  Research Fellow 
*	AS  Research Support 
*	AT  Teaching Support 
*	AO  Senior University Officer 

 

Current wording:

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 - How colleges are represented in the new entity
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

Given the discussion yesterday, can I have access to the currently  PXD 9 level structure for the University? I haven’t seen it with the “padding” levels before yesterday, and then only briefly on screen.

Thanks for spotting the missing L2 full names, I will fix that

Best wishes

Anne

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 14 April 2026 11:39
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: DTP1092 - How colleges are represented in the new entity

 

Hi Anne,

Thank you for putting this together.

I’ve reviewed both proposals and would suggest proceeding with Proposal 1, as it follows the same structure as PACS.

I’ve noted Simon’s points on data warehouse code mapping and potential padding levels. If anyone has any further input before we close this, please let me know.

I noticed Mansfield and Harris Manchester College appear to be missing the L2 full name in the Proposal 1 sheet. Does this need to be corrected?

If there are no further comments, are you happy to take this forward directly with Conor?

Best,

emails.subject: RE: Update
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

 

As discussed yesterday, please see my response below. I wanted to provide some additional context and an update of where we currently are with these items. 

 

Health Surveillance – Automated Emails

The Health Surveillance items listed are all related and sit within the same automated email configuration. This is an area of the system that was not available out‑of‑the‑box and was implemented using a significant amount of custom logic to meet the UOHS’ requirements.

 

To give an indication of the complexity involved:

*	We currently have around 44 business rules and 123 sub‑queries (reports) supporting the automated email functionality.
*	These rules cover 26 different SEG groups, each with their own conditions and email templates.
*	The logic needs to remain consistent across all SEGs, with the exception of Baseline and Recall SEGs, which adds another layer of complexity.
*	Even relatively small changes can have knock‑on effects across multiple business rules using the same sub-queries, so any updates require careful review, documentation, and thorough testing across all scenarios.

 

It’s also important to note that, while we have tried to cover the majority of scenarios in which a client should receive an automated email, there may be a very small number of scenarios that were not captured. In these instances, the email would not be sent and the business rule conditions would need to be reviewed and updated accordingly.

 

I’ve

emails.subject: Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I hope you're well.
I am writing to escalate Case 68351079 (HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues), as we have not received any confirmed resolution.
This was raised by Michael O'Sullivan on 13 February 2026 and relates to two issues within the HESA Module. Data load updates with NULL values are being rejected for records not active in the current HESA return (2024/25), with errors stating the records do not exist in the current year, which is unexpected given the LEVELS UDF operates as a static repository. In addition, there is no clear method to bulk delete UDF entries via data load where the requirement is to remove rows entirely rather than overwrite with NULL.
The case has been followed up multiple times by Michael and progressed via Maura McGlynn, and I understand it has been raised with Development. However, there is no indication of progress or timeframe, and the issue remains unresolved as of today.
As this has now been ongoing for approximately two months, this is becoming urgent. Could you provide an update on the current status, and confirm what progress has been made with Development, including an expected timeframe for resolution?
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 13 April 2026 11:58
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Payroll Validation Failing_pay140

 

We’ve received the latest update from Access Group’s development team regarding the ongoing pay140 payroll validation failure.

 

Access have confirmed that a recent change relating to output messages for tiered pension schemes is generating an unexpectedly high volume of background messages. Because Oxford has particularly large pay groups, this volume is exceeding system limits and causing payroll validation to fail.

 

To resolve this, Access will:

 

•             Deploy a fix to our UAT environment first so we can verify the solution

•             Once confirmed, deploy the same fix to LIVE

 

Their developers are actively working on this, and Access will provide a further update — including any hotfix scheduling — as soon as they have more information.

 

I’ll continue to keep everyone updated as this progresses.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan 
Sent: 1

Hi All

Following our meeting this morning, we have a couple of proposals for the structure of colleges entity which try to mirror the PACS org structure as far as possible, but put the colleges at L3, so we would be reporting consistently at the same org structure level for the whole population. This, or course, assumes that we do not have to set each College up as a separate company within the multi-company structure.

Best wishes

Anne

emails.subject: Urgent request for an SQL report please to help identify balance code
emails.body:
Hi Kevin, Michael,

I am hoping someone from your team could help us please with an SQL report, it is quite urgent. 

We have three very large areas left to go live and we need to complete a balance adjustment for these areas to ensure their leave is correct for their staff from go live. We need to complete this within the next couple of weeks. 

In order to ease the manual piece of this work, and speed up the process, we wanted to know if it was possible/ you had capacity to create an SQL report that pulled the name, personnel ID, appt ID and balance code from PeopleXD for these depts (two of them are divisions).

We have reports that pull the balance description but then we need to manually match that to the balance matrix in order to get the balance code to use in the data upload files.

I have spoken to Simon who said this is possible using SQL developer and to reach out to you both to take forward. Simon is happy to advise if you need any assistance. 

Please let us know ASAP, if this is possible and when it could be achieved by. Your help is greatly appreciated to get these last depts over this final hurdle. 

Many thanks,

Anna



Anna Carter-Windle

Business Change Manager

Contractor of AL Training Solutions Ltd

 

Workforce Management resources for HR, staff and managers <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/workforce-management-resources-hr-staff-managers.aspx> 

 

Programme and Project Deli

emails.subject: RE: Proposed security model for annual leave tool
emails.to: Athena Artuso; Simon Burford; Elena Jarii; Beth Gray
emails.body:
Hi Simon

 

I agree with Athena, with only consideration being around sensitive departments.

 

That said, the security we apply should be led by the users, so can we capture a general consensus on these two options during our engagement activity. If it is option 2, then we will need to consider in more detail and how to ensure protecting those in sensitive departments.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 09 April 2026 11:05
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Elena Jarii <elena.jarii@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Proposed security model for annual leave tool

 

HI Simon,

 

My initial feeling is that if seeing a staff member’s total appointments in-year would give a workgroup manager a better understanding of how that staff member’s leave is calculated, then we should be giving managers access to that information. It would forestall potential questions if the information was already available, making the annual leave tool more useful in general. 

 

Is there a compelling reason to hide appointments in other work groups? The only thing I can think of is sensitive departments.

 

Best,

Athena

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

Hi All,

 

As we move into April, I wanted to give you an update of some of the work that has been going on in the background in support of your systems. Please feel free to share this with your teams for awareness.

 

Highlights

 

*	Enhancement lifecycle launched – framework presented to key contacts within OH&S and the Incident Reporting POG
*	Launch of the Enhancement Request Form on OSM to allow users to propose new or updates to existing system functionality – Available on OSM here <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Modules/SelfService/#serviceCatalog/filter/AC6143F9458A44F9B3B86C45FF1E571F> 
*	Launch of the Health & Safety Roadmap dashboard to allow all staff to have visibility of enhancement requests that have been delivered, those in progress and planned for the future – Available here <https://app.powerbi.com/groups/me/apps/c00c2bb4-2038-4318-bc17-5e700b6a3927/reports/314ba831-839f-4f53-846d-36acabb3f703/69e9b07a2ba77205059d?ctid=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91&experience=power-bi> 
*	Updates to the  intranet pages for the health and safety systems <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OxIntranet-health-safety-wellbeing/SitePages/health-and-safety-systems.aspx>  with information covering the enhancement lifecycle, H&S Roadmap and, support and engagement arrangements.

emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

Thanks - glad it was a useful session. I have copied you into my email to Conor — he will be in touch to confirm proposed session dates, and I've asked him to direct scheduling coordination to you.

A few things to pick up ahead of the kick-off.

Hierarchy Setup — Internal Review Required
Conor has confirmed that a separate organisation entity will be created for the college staff group and is currently checking internally on best practice for the hierarchy structure. Before configuration begins, we need to complete our own internal review to determine how colleges should be represented within that structure and what reference data and hierarchy elements will need to be created. Could you arrange a session to work through this before the kick-off?

Menu Profile Access Levels
Before Conor can configure the menu profile, we need to define what access levels are required. Could you work with Anne, Nathan, and Nick to pull this together and feed back to me so I can brief Conor ahead of that session?

Downstream Systems — BizTalk, CUD and other identity/HR integrations.
It was flagged in the meeting that the new non-payroll entity will need to be safely excluded from downstream systems and other identity/HR integrations. Could you investigate what's needed on our side to ensure the new entity doesn't get unintentionally picked up by any existing integrations, and feed back to me? This needs to be resolved before configuration begins.

Data Sharing Agree

I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and need to understand what children's data we process for pension payments (e.g. orphan pensions following death in service).

emails.subject: Retention (applicant vs personnel) - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and, need your sign-off on the retention wording. There are two separate areas:
Applicant data — Clear-down process being implemented (covered in my separate erasure document). DPIA states records cleared per retention framework. 
Personnel/leaver data — The DPIA currently states we delete records 7 years after employment ends, but PeopleXD has no functionality to do this. Access Group has developed data minimisation scripts with Glasgow University (clearing bank details, next of kin, photos, etc.) which will eventually become a configurable module — but there's no confirmed timeline for availability, and we'd need to define our retention rules once it exists.
Proposed DPIA wording:
"Personnel records are retained in accordance with University of Oxford retention policy. Automated deletion functionality for leaver records is not currently available within PeopleXD. Access Group is developing data minimisation tooling which, once available, will enable systematic data retention processes to be implemented."
Please can you confirm you are happy with this approach?
Best, 
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,

I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and want to strengthen the section on why we process HR data.

Simon suggested linking data quality to strategic outcomes — specifically how accurate HR data supports things like HESA returns, REF submissions, and Athena Swan accreditation, and how these directly affect funding and our ability to attract talent.

Could you give me a couple of sentences I can include that ties this together? Something along the lines of how accurate data underpins external reporting which influences funding and institutional reputation.

Happy to discuss if easier.

Best, 
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

Hi both,

Ahead of your customer success review with Access Group tomorrow, I wanted to flag two outstanding cases that need raising.

Case 68351079 – HESA Module LEVELS UDF (Case Owner: Maura McGlynn): We raised data cleardown and update issues on 17 February. The data output was shared with the dev team on 4 March by Maura. Her last update was on 19 March, apologising for the delay and confirming she was chasing. We've had nothing back since. Case 68388326 – Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email (Case Owner: Iulian Rosia): We've been trying to resolve a configuration issue with the vacancy alert email since 27 January (original Ref 68285965). Changes applied in our test environments didn't take effect, so a follow-up case was raised on 25 February and passed to the dev team on 2 March. I chased Sarah Murphy on 19 March (cc Mike West) but have had no update. This is now almost two months outstanding.


Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: People XD data question
emails.body:
Hi Roy,

Unfortunately, the Country field in PeopleXD is mandatory and must be populated from the drop-down list — there is no option to set it to Null/blank. The only available choices are specific countries or "Not Known." So, it maybe in this case, "Not Known" is a legitimate value rather than a data-entry error. That said, I appreciate most records will have an actual country selected.

Given this constraint, I think the best route would be a mapping adjustment on the CRM side to handle "Not Known", even if it only applies to a handful of records.

Happy to discuss further if that would help.

Best,
Kevin



________________________________

From: Roy McKee <roy.mckee@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 1:10 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: People XD data question 

Hi Kevin, 

I spoke to a couple of staff from the central HR team. 

They suggested that it was unusual for a staff record to have the country field in their address populated with 'Country Unknown'. 

Would you be able to assist us by asking the owner of this data to change the value in this field from "Not Known" to Null, to match the other address fields? 

We feel this would be an appropriate solution given that this record appears to be an outlier. 


Many thanks, 


Roy McKee
Dynamics Functional Analyst - CRM

Software Solutions | IT Services | University of Oxford

+44 01865 111716
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellin

emails.subject: Re: Rollout of Automatic Annual Leave Carryover
emails.to: Tracy Barlow-Bacon; Simon Burford; Michelle Williams; Michael O'Sullivan
emails.body:
Hi all,

Thanks for today's session, here's a summary of what we covered and the agreed next steps.

Approach
We'll clone the existing holiday scheme and keep the majority of departments on the original, which will have carryover automation enabled (max 5 days). Any departments that opt out will be moved to the cloned scheme with no automation. This avoids moving the majority and keeps manual adjustment work to a minimum. Carryover is set against the balance calendar, not by department. So, this will effectively be a universal setting on the original scheme. Departments that opt out can still manage carryover manually as they do now — just in reverse.

Policy and Process Implications
Melanie (business analyst) discussed this with the policy / wellbeing team, and they did not raise any concerns about automating carryover. Given that policy guidance emphasises using leave and supporting wellbeing, and this would be a universal change, it is recommended we do a follow-up check-in with Kate Butler to confirm before proceeding.

Going forward, we need to involve Julie to define central processes and year-end comms. This includes defining how managers agree the number of days to carry, how they monitor and report on carryover, and providing guidance for departments that want tighter control.

Testing and Timelines
We need a new balance/calendar set up purely for testing. Julie normally starts rollover comms in July, so all configuration, testing, policy sign-off, and c

emails.subject: Follow-up: Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email Configuration Issue (Refs 68285965 & 68388326)
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I hope you're well.
I wanted to follow up on the support ticket 68388326. Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email – Configuration Changes Not Applying, as I have not received any recent updates on this issue.
This relates to an original case (Ref 68285965) which was logged on 27 January to confirm whether the opening text of the system-generated vacancy alert email is configurable.
Following this, we applied the changes in our test environments (OUXU and OUXZ), but the opening text in the outgoing email did not update.
The case (Ref 68388326) was then logged on 25 February to address this, and as of 2 March the issue had been passed to the DEV team. Since then, I have not received any further updates, despite requesting this.
As this has now been ongoing since 27 January 2026, and with the current case now open for over three weeks, this has now become urgent. emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 ORCIDs in Onboarding
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,
Following your email, I’ve reviewed the requirements and wanted to set out what we can support within PeopleXD.
We can capture ORCID during onboarding and allow this to be entered or amended via self-service. The value can also be passed to the data warehouse alongside SSO.
We can also control which staff groups are presented with the field during onboarding. However, this cannot be enforced beyond that point, as the field would remain available in self-service, and users or HR teams would still be able to enter or amend it outside of onboarding.
There are elements of the requirements that we cannot support. Prepopulating the field from CUD would require a new integration into PeopleXD, which is not in place. Validation via orcid.org would also require external integration and is not supported within PeopleXD.
It would be helpful to understand the benefit of capturing ORCID given that we cannot verify whether the ORCID entered is correct, meaning entries could include typos, incorrect values, or another person’s ORCID, which would not be identified within the system.
If you’re able to share any further context on priorities, timelines, or the original business case, that would help us to support next steps.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: Re: Asta managing HR chat members
emails.body:
Hi Julie,

I’ve been added to the Teams channel to understand the context. It appears this is not a Teams chat, but a channel primarily used to access the WFM weekly rotating schedule finder and some historic Mobile App - go live announcements. Given this, I don’t think overall ownership should sit with the FA team. Would it be more appropriate for this to sit with Business Change?

Of the two options, the second approach, providing users with the option to request access – sounds reasonable. Asta is comfortable supporting this from a membership management perspective. We would need to agree a clear process, defined eligibility, and ensure there is more than one approver to avoid a single point of failure, with this managed by the owner.

It would be helpful to arrange a short meeting to confirm ownership, responsibilities, and the agreed approach so we are all aligned before moving forward.

Best,
Kevin

I’ve indicated that the opt-in approach sounds reasonable, with you supporting membership management. I’ve also noted that we would need a clearly agreed process, defined eligibility, and more than one approver to avoid a single point of failure, with oversight sitting with the confirmed owner.

emails.subject: Re: WFM Rollout Timing – ESS Alignment
emails.body:
Hi Tracy,
Following our recent discussion, I understand there are ongoing conversations regarding an April go-live for GLAM and SBS.
At the last meeting, we had pencilled in 13 March for the ESS interface go-live, with the intention that menu profiles would be merged ahead of this so access aligned with the interface deployment.
Given the shift in timeline, could you confirm whether this impacts the planned go-live of the ESS interface and do you still anticipate we can proceed with 13 March, or will this now need to move into April?
It would be helpful to confirm so we can plan accordingly.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 4:16 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: WFM Rollout Timing – ESS Alignment 

Hi

From project board today, we are going to have a push for all departments to be live at the latest in March.  We do need the API to be live before the end of the project which is April as it was one of the deliverables.  The vast majority have adopted the system and we have several departments going live early March so if you had an 'ideal' then I would say mid-end March as we are really hoping to have most departments live by then.  If there are any hangovers at that point it will be the minority.

Hope that helps?

Thanks
Tracy

emails.subject: TIMDEP Go-Live – HR Bulletin Content
emails.body:
Hi Michelle,
We are ready to proceed with the TIMDEP and CoreTime report updates and wanted to know if we should include this in the next HR Bulletin.
There are a few key points to highlight in the communication:

*	
	Report renames:
	“TIMDEP02 Casuals Only – Leave Entitlement” will be renamed to
	“TIMDEP02 Leave Entitlement for Accruing Casual and Variable Hours Staff (v4)”.
*	
	Security model alignment:
	The reports will now align with People Management security. Users may notice differences in results compared to previous runs, as data will reflect their system security permissions.
*	
	Historic absence cut-off:
	TIMDEP03 Sickness Absence Reasons (v4) and TIMDEP04 Absence Reasons (v2) will only return absence records with a start date on or after 1 August 2021.

Could you confirm when you would like this to go live so we can align the bulletin timing with the change implementation?
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

emails.subject: Re: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention
emails.to: Data Protection; HR Systems Support; Marie Cooksey
emails.body:
Hi Joshua,
Thank you for your email. Please see our responses below.
Erasure of Unsuccessful Application Data
We are in the process of deleting Vanessa Beaumont’s unsuccessful application records that fall outside the six-month retention window. We will confirm once this has been completed.
6-Month vs 26-Month Retention
We acknowledge the misalignment and are addressing this as part of our wider applicant data retention review. Our guidance is being aligned with the University retention schedule, and we will confirm the agreed position shortly.
PeopleXD Cleardown Process
The automated cleardown process in PeopleXD is not currently live. Testing is underway and subject to business change considerations, a realistic go-live timeline is likely to be within the next few months.
In the interim, manual erasure requests will continue to be processed in line with the retention schedule.
Cross-Department Application History — Access and Safeguards
We have reviewed the  PeopleXD system access and can confirm:

*	
	Visibility in PeopleXD is department specific. Users can only view applications for departments to which they have access.
*	
	Cross-department visibility is restricted to users who hold both a recruitment administrator menu profile and recruitment system user/HR access. This is a limited group and does not apply to general departmental HR staff.
*	
	Access is controlled through system access groups and restricted to recruiting administrators. There are no ad

Hi Both,

Crispin has asked whether we are able to contribute FlexPoints towards the Level 2 support for the College Data initiative, so I think we should align internally before responding.

Level 2 is approximately £18,000 incl. VAT (c. £15,400 ex VAT / 9,800 FlexPoints). Assuming the project’s proposed £11,000 contribution is inclusive of VAT, we would need to cover approximately 4,000 FlexPoints to make up the remaining balance until renewal on 1 July.

We currently have 9,410 FlexPoints available. This does not take into account any upcoming HESA-related work, which may also require FlexPoint allocation.

Can we agree what level of contribution we are comfortable making, given existing commitments and anticipated demand?

Happy to discuss.

Best,
Kevin

Hi Marie,
I agree with James’ response.
There was no formal agreement following our follow-up meeting with Ralph and Claire that HR Systems would take ownership of the manual uploads.
The proposed interim method would be to add new starters to the most recent spreadsheet rather than generate a completely new full extract each time, to minimise the risk of unintended changes.
I have advised Ralph that we have a monthly report covering new starters within a defined date range. This will help quantify the changes required each month.
As outlined in James’ email, the question of where this responsibility will sit remains open, with Ralph confirming he would check Safety Office admin capacity.
Best,
Kevin

As the error appears to be occurring at parameter level rather than within the SQL itself, could you please enable “Allow null value” for the Personnel Number parameter in the test environment and re-run the report with Personnel Number left blank.
The SQL is written to handle a NULL value and this aligns with the published guidance, so this will confirm whether the current behaviour is being driven by the parameter configuration.
Let me know if you need my help.
Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: Action raised in CDR/Professional Development meeting this morning
emails.body:
Hi Lindsey,

Apologies for not providing this update sooner. Following the meeting with Access Group Development regarding the Talent template permissions, I can confirm the outcome. 

Access Group reviewed the configuration and confirmed there is currently no functionality available to restrict edit or clone permissions separately within Talent. Permissions to the Talent Admin dashboard is effectively all-or-nothing, and templates cannot be made read-only for some users while retaining edit rights for others.

As such, there is no system fix currently available. In the absence of a technical solution, the mitigation is user education and clear guidance on how templates should be cloned and assigned.

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: College Data – Recommended Support Level and Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

Following our discussion with Simon and the team, and review of Access Group’s clarifications, we recommend proceeding with Level 2 support for the College Data non-payroll company setup.

While Level 1 is technically workable, it would leave menu profile configuration, ESS account setup, and associated access administration to be delivered internally. Given the complexity of menu profile design, this would require dedicated internal resource to manage. Level 2 provides support in these areas while retaining internal control of the company structure and hierarchy configuration.

Although Level 3 includes additional configuration and impact review support, configuration ownership and downstream validation would still require our oversight. The additional cost does not provide sufficient added value beyond Level 2.
Access Group have confirmed that review of alerts and reporting behaviour has not been included within the scope in Level 2, but can be included if required.

We recommend that the following are explicitly included within project scope:

*	
	Formal downstream impact assessment (alerts, reporting, interfaces, data warehouse feeds)
*	
	A project UAT testing resource, with provision of dedicated mobile hardware for app testing
*	
	A project nominated administrative resource (separate from the tester) to manage account setup, access maintenance, and day-to-day administration following go-live


Please let me know if you require any further d

emails.subject: Re: ORCID - Progress with Access Work
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,
Following the Proof of Concept with Access Group, the scripted task clear-down approach has been validated and documented.
As outlined in my January update, delivery follows the agreed sequence of steps. While the technical approach is complete, the ORCID checklist requirements still require formal confirmation and sign-off by business owners.
We now need to finalise the checklist requirements (including wording, mandatory status, guidance text, validation, and reporting considerations) before a test deployment can be scheduled with Access Group.
Delivery by the end of February was considered achievable provided the work was scheduled promptly. As the requirements have not yet been confirmed, deployment has not been scheduled and it is therefore now unlikely that delivery can be achieved by the end of February.
As this step sits with DTP1092/business owners, a short session to confirm the requirements would allow us to move into scheduling with Access Group. I am happy to support from a technical perspective once that is arranged.
Best,
Kevin

As we move into year-end planning, I wanted to check whether there are any updates from Access Group regarding this year’s year-end release. I haven’t yet seen any communication and wanted to ensure we haven’t missed anything.
We would be keen to participate as a pilot or early adopter again, if that is being offered this year.
Could you also confirm what key changes are expected in the upcoming release and whether there is an indicative timeline for communications or briefings?
@Jennifer – we will need to have advance notice of any changes to pension rates that we’ll also need to build into the year end process, so could you ask your teams to get in touch as soon as any changes are known?

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

emails.subject: Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.to: AnnMarie Topliss; Amy Coogan; Mike West
emails.body:
Hi Ann-Marie,

Following your recent email to Marie, we have reviewed the L1 and L2 scope options for the non-pay employee implementation. I’d like to clarify a few points before we confirm the level of support.

Could you please confirm the following in terms of Access Group responsibility under Level 1 and Level 2 support:
Data loading

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to data loading — does this include execution of data loads and investigation/resolution of load failures, or is support only limited to training and review?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, what additional responsibility does Access Group take in relation to data loading and any issue resolution?

Configuration

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to configuration — does this include applying configuration changes (e.g. hierarchy items and reference data) in UAT and Live, or is support limited to training and guidance?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group take responsibility for configuration activities such as the Non-Pay Pay Group setup, and are there any additional configuration responsibilities included?

Test accounts

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to test account creation and management — is this activity included within L1 support?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group take responsibility for test account setup and ongoing management during U

emails.subject: Re: Quick Q on My Development functionality: Annual CDR and professional development data for the Concordat Action Plan
emails.body:
Hi Rachel, Lindsey
Apologies for the delayed reply.
Yes - it is technically possible for departments to use My Development to log professional development activity only, without using the P/CDR elements of the module.
However, My Development was implemented to support Career Development Reviews for Researchers, and any use outside that core purpose would be unsupported beyond the existing published guidance.
In relation to the Concordat data, departments using My Development can access standard reports within PeopleXD/Insight to view the information they have recorded. However, we are not able to pull reports on behalf of departments or map system data to align with the questions used in the survey.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

This question came up in Research Staff Working Group because several departments are understandably unwilling t

emails.subject: Re: WFM Rollout Timing – ESS Alignment
emails.body:
Hi Tracy,
Thanks for the update.
Yes, this relates to the ESS API. Do you have a view on when you would prefer this to go live, given the decision pending on the two larger areas?
The integration team is ready to proceed. From our side, our preference would be to align go-live with completion of the final WFM department rollouts, to avoid manual workload and reduce additional support queries for departments not yet live.
Happy to have a quick call to discuss once you have an update on the decision.
Best,
Kevin

Subject: Re: WFM Rollout Timing – ESS Alignment

Is this the ESS API?  It is part of the WFM project so I need to keep updated on rollout as I need to report back to board on this.

In terms of departments going live, we envisage the last one's to go live in March at the latest and we're aiming for early March with those.  However, I have board today as we're waiting on a decision regarding 2 big areas who are requesting a go live later this year after the project team are no longer here so I can update you once that decision is made.

Kind regards
Tracy

emails.subject: Re: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.
emails.to: Tony Boydell; David Hickman; Erika Braverman; Andrew Goff (IT Services); Simon Burford; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi Tony,
Apologies for the delayed response.
@David Hickman <mailto:david.hickman@it.ox.ac.uk>  – thanks for the detail in your email below.
Following our internal discussions, we would appreciate your team’s support in helping us gain assurance around the API’s initial live deprovisioning behaviour. Specifically, could you provide an output showing the list of records that would be deprovisioned on the first live run, without performing the deprovisioning itself? This would allow us to validate first-run outcomes and confirm there is no unexpected loss of access when the API is enabled in Live.
Once we’ve reviewed that output, we’ll be in a position to confirm next steps and timing from our side.
Happy to discuss if helpful, but hopefully this clarifies what we’re asking for.
Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
I think it’s helpful to separate two distinct but related points: API readiness and an internal HR Systems operational dependency.
As discussed with Asta, from an API perspective she’s comfortable with the solution and the work completed to date gives confidence in production readiness. The outstanding point, as left in the meeting with Tony, was whether any additional assurance is required around end-to-end behaviour in Live, including clear visibility of provisioning and deprovisioning outcomes. That distinction does not yet appear to be fully reflected in the thread, and I think it’s important we align internally before we respond.
The issue that remains is operational. ESS notification emails are still sent manually, and today that process is triggered using an internal daily list of newly provisioned users. Once provisioning becomes automated via the API, that visibility disappears unless a replacement daily list is in place.
If that list isn’t available, there is no way to identify who the notification email should be sent to. In that scenario, users would not receive the expected email and access queries would surface via First Line, without a clear way to confirm which accounts have been provisioned.
Given this directly affects our ability to operate on day one, I think it’s sensible for us to align internally on a plan before responding further. I’ve sent a short meeting invite for 1pm tomorrow to talk this through.
Best,
Kevin


_________________

emails.subject: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.
emails.to: Tony Boydell; Erika Braverman; Andrew Goff (IT Services); Simon Burford; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi all,

I wanted to set out the current UAT position, the residual risks that remain, and the assurance we believe should be required before providing, in writing, final sign-off for go-live.
From a formal testing perspective, Asta is content with the scenarios that have been executed to date. API testing against manually selected records has completed successfully, and the outcomes align with expectations. There are no concerns with the logic as demonstrated under these controlled conditions.
The remaining risks relate to production behaviour that has not been fully exercised in UAT. In Live, the API will run autonomously and identify eligible records based on rule-based criteria. That end-to-end, rule-driven behaviour has not been replicated in the test environment, as doing so would require additional build work that is currently out of scope and unfunded. As ESS deprovisioning has not been exercised in a bulk, rule-driven context, this introduces a risk of unexpected outcomes when eligibility changes are processed automatically in Live. There is also no confirmed operational reporting in place for HR that clearly identifies which records have been created or updated by the API.
Before providing final sign-off, we believe further assurance would be required either through end-to-end, rule-based testing that mirrors production behaviour (including deprovisioning), or through clear operational reporting that provides visibility of API-driven activity. Alternatively, if

emails.subject: Re: #External# PeopleXD SMTP change – UAT readiness and confirmation
emails.body:
Hi Mike,
I have been advised by Chris on support - ticket 68208871, to chase this with you to see whether a consultant can be engaged to support the UAT and go live properly for the SMTP change.
For clarity, I’ve summarised the current position below.
1. What has been completed so far
Two event items have been configured to send notifications to me alone on the system:

*	
	PRA003-001 (HR Event Configuration)
*	
	LR100 (Process Configuration)

These can be found in the HR Event Configuration and Process Configuration areas respectively.
2. EMS configuration and email queue
We can see a large number of existing items in the email queue in People Management. While reducing the history length would normally limit how long items remain in the queue, Chris has advised this is not guaranteed to clear the queue entirely, as additional items may still be generated that fall outside the scope of the testing. This is a concern as we are actively trying to prevent notifications being sent to unintended recipients. As a result, we are unable to proceed with UAT until the queue has been cleared.
3. What is still required to complete testing and proceed to Live.
As far as we are aware, the remaining step is to test the two configured event items and confirm they successfully land in my mailbox to validate the change. These cover three of our previously stated requirements (attachment, external email, and internal email).
In parallel, support ticket 68296059 remains open, wh

Hi Marie,

This has been escalated to both The Access Group and IT Services.
Unfortunately, this is a convoluted BT legacy setup, and IT Services are not taking ownership of the issue.

The Access Group’s recommended route is to implement the DKIM setup, as this is likely to resolve the problem.

I was scheduled to carry out UAT today, but this has been rescheduled by The Access Group. I am contacting Mike to raise this with the Dev team, with the aim of having the DKIM changes implemented next week.

I will provide an update once timelines are confirmed.

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: ESS Formal Integration Test Scenarios
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Just to add to Asta’s email and clarify where we landed - Asta was asked to set out in writing her acceptance position for UAT — confirming she is content with the scenarios already tested, and clearly documenting what further assurance she would need before final sign-off for go-live, including the specific risks she is seeking to mitigate.
The intention was to make those risks and conditions explicit, so that a clear decision can be taken on whether further funding is sought or whether the remaining risk is accepted and the integration proceeds to Live as it stands.
Best,
Kevin

Hi Kevin 

The Support team will confirm this on the support case but the process from our side on the UAT email situation is as below:


1.	Ensure the test run indicator to "on"
2.	Note the username and email in EMS username and administrator email
3.	Change the email field to a users email address for the test. So likely one of the users involved in this project.
4.	Ensure the client sets the original email back.

emails.subject: Re: ORCID - Progress with Access Work
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

I can confirm that there will be no charge to DTP1092 for this work. Based on your note about the project focus shifting next month, delivery by the end of February is achievable, provided the work is scheduled promptly.

Please see the clarification below:

Objective
Enable the addition of new onboarding checklist fields in PeopleXD (e.g. ORCID) without triggering retrospective onboarding notifications for existing staff. This allows ORCID capture to be introduced safely as part of onboarding.

Steps

*	
	Confirm and document the scripted “task clear-down” approach validated by Access Group (HR Systems / Access Group).
*	
	Finalise the ORCID checklist requirements (DTP1092 / business owners).
*	
	Deploy the new checklist item in PeopleXD onboarding (Access Group).
*	
	Execute scripted clear-down to prevent legacy notifications (Access Group, supported by HR Systems).
*	
	Validate outcome and confirm readiness for use (HR Systems).


Timings

*	
	Scoping and confirmation of requirements: 1 week (Already done but will require final confirmation)
*	
	Scheduling and deployment: 1–2 weeks
*	
	Validation and sign-off: 1 week



Best,
Kevin

Following our discussion, I just wanted to clarify the following point. As the sending address remains peoplexd@accessacloud.com, our Networks and Infrastructure team have advised there is no need to make any DNS changes. SPF for accessacloud.com is owned and managed by Access Group, so confirmation from your side is required rather than an update on ours.
Before we schedule the UAT window, could you please confirm:

	That PeopleXD UAT will be switched to use smtp.accessacloud.com.
*	
	That SPF for accessacloud.com already authorises smtp.accessacloud.com.
*	
	What email safety controls are in place in UAT to prevent unintended notifications during testing.
*	
	When you would be available to support the UAT session.

@Mike West <mailto:Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com> — separately, and outside the scope of this change, could you confirm what Access Group involvement would be required if we decided to move to an @ox.ac.uk sending address in future, including whether this would require flexpoints?

Hi Andy,
Thanks again for confirming, and joining the call tomorrow.
I had one quick question ahead of that. Access Group have confirmed there will also be a DNS update required on the Oxford University side to allow their SMTP service to send on our behalf. This would involve adding the following SPF record:

*	"v=spf1 include:spf2.accessacloud.com ~all"
	and the sending server/IP is:
*	smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>    (212.54.130.143)

Could you advise who in IT Services would normally handle this type of DNS change, and whether this should be raised via a specific OSM request? It would also be useful to know whether someone from that team would typically need to be included in the call, or if this is something we can follow up separately.
Best,
Kevin

emails.body:
Hi Andy,
Just to confirm, would 11:00–11:30 work for you?
DKIM stands for DomainKeys Identified Mail. It’s an email authentication mechanism that allows receiving mail systems to verify that an email was genuinely sent by the specific stated domain and hasn’t been altered.
Best,
Kevin

Ahead of Thursday’s session, I wanted to check whether it would be ok to use a manager Insight report as a practical module to build during the training.

Kevin

emails.subject: Fw: Payroll Module Process Review (Case No: 67285209) Follow-up and Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Mike,

I sent the below to Amanda and wanted to share it with you for awareness.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Friday, January 09, 2026 12:50 PM
To: Amanda Mcdonnell <amanda.mcdonnell@theaccessgroup.com>
Cc: Tracy Roberts <tracy.roberts@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Joanna Taylor <joanna.taylor@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Payroll Module Process Review (Case No: 67285209) Follow-up and Next Steps 

Hi Amanda,
Happy New Year!
It seems this has fallen off our radar, so I wanted to follow up and agree how we progress this.
Following our Payroll Module Process Review session on 20 October 2025, we spent time walking through retrospective appointment and salary changes, pension recalculation behaviour (particularly for tiered schemes), FPS considerations, and payslip handling. The session was helpful in exploring current system behaviour and highlighting areas that would benefit from further review.
As this activity sits under the agreed Payroll Module Health Check, it would be good to now move this forward into a more formal outcome. We would welcome a follow-up review meeting this month to complete the review and formalise the findings, confirm any conclusions on current behaviour versus best practice, and agree any recommendations or next steps.
Please let me know your availability over the coming weeks so we can get a session booked.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

emails.subject: Re: Case 67938206 - The University Of Oxford - Bulk delete assignments
emails.body:
Hi Julie,
I wanted to follow up on the ongoing Talent appraisal template issue we discussed.
I have now cancelled the related change request, as the proposed scripting work would only address the immediate symptom and does not prevent the issue from recurring. At present, there appears to be no mitigation or control available within the product, which leaves us exposed to the same risk.
Here is the quote from AG:

I wanted to check whether there is any additional guidance you would recommend.
Let me know if you wish to proceed as this will require need to be approved by Marie 
Best,
Kevin

Thank you for the update.
At this stage, I need to discuss this internally before deciding how to proceed. I also note that I have not received a formal quote, and at present we will not require one.
That said, could you please provide an indicative view of how many FlexPoints this would be likely to consume? This will allow me to assess whether proceeding represents sufficient value, given the lack of a preventative outcome.
